text: These are the words, which Moses spoke to all Israel beyond the Jordan, in the plain wilderness, over against the Red Sea, between Pharan and Thophel and Laban and Haseroth, where there is very much gold.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Eleven days’ journey from Horeb by the way of mount Seir to Cadesbarne.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: In the fortieth year, the eleventh month, the first day of the month, Moses spoke to the children of Israel all that the Lord had commanded him to say to them:

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: After that he had slain Sehon king of the Amorrhites, who dwelt in Hesebon: and Og king of Basan who abode in Astaroth, and in Edrai,

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Beyond the Jordan in the land of Moab. And Moses began to expound the law, and to say:

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: The Lord our God spoke to us in Horeb, saying: You have stayed long enough in this mountain:

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Turn you, and come to the mountain of the Amorrhites, and to the other places that are next to it, the plains and the hills and the vales towards the south, and by the sea shore, the land of the Chanaanites, and of Libanus, as far as the great river Euphrates.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Behold, said he, I have delivered it to you: go in and possess it, concerning which the Lord swore to your fathers Abraham, Isaac, and Jacob, that he would give it to them, and to their seed after them.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And I said to you at that time:

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: I alone am not able to bear you: for the Lord your God hath multiplied you, and you are this day as the stars of heaven, for multitude.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: (The Lord God of your fathers add to this number many thousands, and bless you as he hath spoken.)

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: I alone am not able to bear your business, and the charge of you and your differences.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Let me have from among you wise and understanding men, and such whose conversation is approved among your tribes, that I may appoint them your rulers.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Then you answered me: The thing is good which thou meanest to do.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And I took out of your tribes men wise and honourable, and appointed them rulers, tribunes, and centurions, and officers over fifties, and over tens, who might teach you all things.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And I commanded them, saying: Hear them, and judge that which is just: whether he be one of your country, or a stranger.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: There shall be no difference of persons, you shall hear the little as well as the great: neither shall you respect any man’s person, because it is the judgment of God. And if any thing seem hard to you, refer it to me, and I will hear it.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And I commanded you all things that you were to do.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And departing from Horeb, we passed through the terrible and vast wilderness, which you saw, by the way of the mountain of the Amorrhite, as the Lord our God had commanded us. And when we were come into Cadesbarne,

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: I said to you: You are come to the mountain of the Amorrhite, which the Lord our God will give to us.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: See the land which the Lord thy God giveth thee: go up and possess it, as the Lord our God hath spoken to thy fathers: fear not, nor be any way discouraged.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And you came all to me, and said: Let us send men who may view the land, and bring us word what way we shall go up, and to what cities we shall go.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And because the saying pleased me, I sent of you twelve men, one of every tribe:

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Who, when they had set forward and had gone up to the mountains, came as far as the valley of the cluster: and having viewed the land,

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Taking of the fruits thereof, to shew its fertility, they brought them to us, and said: The land is good, which the Lord our God will give us.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And you would not go up, but being incredulous to the word of the Lord our God,

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: You murmured in your tents, and said: The Lord hateth us, and therefore he hath brought us out of the land of Egypt, that he might deliver us into the hand of the Amorrhite, and destroy us.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Whither shall we go up? the messengers have terrified our hearts, saying: The multitude is very great, and taller than we: the cities are great, and walled up to the sky, we have seen the sons of the Enacims there.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And I said to you: Fear not, neither be ye afraid of them:

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: The Lord God, who is your leader, himself will fight for you, as he did in Egypt in the sight of all.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And in the wilderness (as thou hast seen) the Lord thy God hath carried thee, as a man is wont to carry his little son, all the way that you have come, until you came to this place.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And yet for all this you did not believe the Lord your God,

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Who went before you in the way, and marked out the place, wherein you should pitch your tents, in the night shewing you the way by fire, and in the day by the pillar of a cloud.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And when the Lord had heard the voice of your words, he was angry and swore, and said:

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Not one of the men of this wicked generation shall see the good land, which I promised with an oath to your fathers:

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Except Caleb the son of Jephone: for he shall see it, and to him I will give the land that he hath trodden upon, and to his children, because he hath followed the Lord.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Neither is his indignation against the people to be wondered at, since the Lord was angry with me also on your account, and said: Neither shalt thou go in thither.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: But Josue the son of Nun, thy minister, he shall go in for thee: exhort and encourage him, and he shall divide the land by lot to Israel.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Your children, of whom you said that they should be led away captives, and your sons who know not this day the difference of good and evil, they shall go in: and to them I will give the land, and they shall possess it.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: But return you and go into the wilderness by the way of the Red Sea.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And you answered me: We have sinned against the Lord: we will go up and fight, as the Lord our God hath commanded. And when you went ready armed unto the mountain,

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: The Lord said to me: Say to them: Go not up, and fight not, for I am not with you: lest you fall before your enemies.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: I spoke, and you hearkened not: but resisting the commandment of the Lord, and swelling with pride, you went up into the mountain.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And the Amorrhite that dwelt in the mountains coming out, and meeting you, chased you, as bees do: and made slaughter of you from Seir as far as Horma.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And when you returned and wept before the Lord, he heard you not, neither would he yield to your voice.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: So you abode in Cadesbarne a long time.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And departing from thence we came into the wilderness that leadeth to the Red Sea, as the Lord had spoken to me: and we compassed mount Seir a long time.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And the Lord said to me:

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: You have compassed this mountain long enough: go toward the north:

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And command thou the people, saying: You shall pass by the borders of your brethren the children of Esau, who dwell in Seir, and they will be afraid of you.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Take ye then good heed that you stir not against them. For I will not give you of their land so much as the step of one foot can tread upon, because I have given mount Seir to Esau, for a possession.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: You shall buy meats of them for money and shall eat: you shall draw waters for money, and shall drink.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: The Lord thy God hath blessed thee in every work of thy hands: the Lord thy God dwelling with thee, knoweth thy journey, how thou hast passed through this great wilderness, for forty years, and thou hast wanted nothing.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And when we had passed by our brethren the children of Esau, that dwelt in Seir, by the way of the plain from Elath and from Asiongaber, we came to the way that leadeth to the desert of Moab.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And the Lord said to me: Fight not against the Moabites, neither go to battle against them: for I will not give thee any of their land, because I have given Ar to the children of Lot in possession.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: The Emims first were the inhabitants thereof, a people great, and strong, and so tall, that like the race of the Enacims,

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: They were esteemed as giants, and were like the sons of the Enacims. But the Moabites call them Emims.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: The Horrhites also formerly dwelt in Seir: who being driven out and destroyed, the children of Esau dwelt there, as Israel did in the land of his possession, which the Lord gave him.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Then rising up to pass the torrent Zared, we came to it.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And the time that we journeyed from Cadesbarne till we passed over the torrent Zared, was thirty-eight years: until all the generation of the men that were fit for war was consumed out of the camp, as the Lord had sworn:

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: For his hand was against them, that they should perish from the midst of the camp.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And after all the fighting men were dead,

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: The Lord spoke to me, saying:

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Thou shalt pass this day the borders of Moab, the city named Ar:

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And when thou comest nigh the frontiers of the children of Ammon, take heed thou fight not against them, nor once move to battle: for I will not give thee of the land of the children of Ammon, because I have given it to the children of Lot for a possession.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: It was accounted a land of giants: and giants formerly dwelt in it, whom the Ammonites call Zomzommims,

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: A people great and many, and of tall stature, like the Enacims whom the Lord destroyed before their face: and he made them to dwell in their stead,

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: As he had done in favour of the children of Esau, that dwell in Seir, destroying the Horrhites, and delivering their land to them, which they possess to this day.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: The Hevites also, that dwelt in Haserim as far as Gaza, were expelled by the Cappadocians: who came out of Cappadocia, and destroyed them and dwelt in their stead.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Arise ye, and pass the torrent Arnon: Behold I have delivered into thy hand Sehon king of Hesebon the Amorrhite, and begin thou to possess his land and make war against him.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: This day will I begin to send the dread and fear of thee upon the nations that dwell under the whole heaven: that when they hear thy name they may fear and tremble, and be in pain like women in travail.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: So I sent messengers from the wilderness of Cademoth to Sehon the king of Hesebon with peaceable words, saying:

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: We will pass through thy land, we will go along by the highway: we will not turn aside neither to the right hand nor to the left.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Sell us meat for money, that we may eat: give us water for money and so we will drink. We only ask that thou wilt let us pass through,

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: As the children of Esau have done, that dwell in Seir, and the Moabites, that abide in Ar: until we come to the Jordan, and pass to the land which the Lord our God will give us.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And Sehon the king of Hesebon would not let us pass: because the Lord thy God had hardened his spirit, and fixed his heart, that he might be delivered into thy hands, as now thou seest.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And the Lord said to me: Behold I have begun to deliver unto thee Sehon and his land, begin to possess it.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And Sehon came out to meet us with all his people to fight at Jasa.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And the Lord our God delivered him to us: and we slew him with his sons and all his people.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And we took all his cities at that time, killing the inhabitants of them, men and women and children. We left nothing of them:

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Except the cattle which came to the share of them that took them: and the spoils of the cities, which we took:

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: From Aroer, which is upon the bank of the torrent Arnon, a town that is situate in a valley, as far as Galaad. There was not a village or city, that escaped our hands: the Lord our God delivered all unto us:

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Except the land of the children of Ammon, to which we approached not: and all that border upon the torrent Jeboc, and the cities in the mountains, and all the places which the Lord our God forbade us.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Then we turned and went by the way of Basan: and Og the king of Basan came out to meet us with his people to fight in Edrai.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And the Lord said to me: Fear him not: because he is delivered into thy hand, with all his people and his land: and thou shalt do to him as thou hast done to Sehon king of the Amorrhites, that dwelt in Hesebon.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: So the Lord our God delivered into our hands, Og also, the king of Basan, and all his people: and we utterly destroyed them,

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Wasting all his cities at one time, there was not a town that escaped us: sixty cities, all the country of Argob the kingdom of Og in Basan.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: All the cities were fenced with very high walls, and with gates and bars, besides innumerable towns that had no walls.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And we utterly destroyed them, as we had done to Sehon the king of Hesebon, destroying every city, men and women and children:

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: But the cattle and the spoils of the cities we took for our prey.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And we took at that time the land out of the hand of the two kings of the Amorrhites, that were beyond the Jordan: from the torrent Arnon unto the mount Hermon,

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Which the Sidonians call Sarion, and the Amorrhites Sanir:

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: All the cities that are situate in the plain, and all the land of Galaad and Basan as far as Selcha and Edrai, cities of the kingdom of Og in Basan.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: For only Og king of Basan remained of the race of the giants. His bed of iron is shewn, which is in Rabbath of the children of Ammon, being nine cubits long, and four broad after the measure of the cubit of a man’s hand.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And we possessed the land at that time from Aroer, which is upon the bank of the torrent Arnon, unto the half of mount Galaad: and I gave the cities thereof to Ruben and Gad.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And I delivered the other part of Galaad, and all Basan the kingdom of Og to the half tribe of Manasses, all the country of Argob: and all Basan is called the Land of giants.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Jair the son of Manasses possessed all the country of Argob unto the borders of Gessuri, and Machati. And he called Basan by his own name, Havoth Jair, that is to say, the towns of Jair, until this present day.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: To Machir also I gave Galaad.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And to the tribes of Ruben and Gad I gave of the land of Galaad as far as the torrent Arnon, half the torrent, and the confines even unto the torrent Jeboc, which is the border of the children of Ammon:

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And the plain of the wilderness, and the Jordan, and the borders of Cenereth unto the sea of the desert, which is the most salt sea, to the foot of mount Phasga eastward.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And I commanded you at that time, saying: The Lord your God giveth you this land for an inheritance, go ye well appointed before your brethren the children of Israel, all the strong men of you.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Leaving your wives and children and cattle. For I know you have much cattle, and they must remain in the cities, which I have delivered to you.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Until the Lord give rest to your brethren, as he hath given to you: and they also possess the land, which he will give them beyond the Jordan: then shall every man return to his possession, which I have given you.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: I commanded Josue also at that time, saying: Thy eyes have seen what the Lord your God hath done to these two kings: so will he do to all the kingdoms to which thou shalt pass.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Fear them not: for the Lord your God will fight for you.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And I besought the Lord at that time, saying:

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Lord God, thou hast begun to shew unto thy servant thy greatness, and most mighty hand, for there is no other God either in heaven or earth, that is able to do thy works, or to be compared to thy strength.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: I will pass over therefore, and will see this excellent land beyond the Jordan, and this goodly mountain, and Libanus.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And the Lord was angry with me on your account and heard me not, but said to me: It is enough: speak no more to me of this matter.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Go up to the top of Phasga, and cast thy eyes round about to the west, and to the north, and to the south, and to the east, and behold it, for thou shalt not pass this Jordan.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Command Josue, and encourage and strengthen him: for he shall go before this people, and shall divide unto them the land which thou shalt see.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And we abode in the valley over against the temple of Phogor.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And now, O Israel, hear the commandments and judgments which I teach thee: that doing them, thou mayst live, and entering in mayst possess the land which the Lord the God of your fathers will give you.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: You shall not add to the word that I speak to you, neither shall you take away from it: keep the commandments of the Lord your God which I command you.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Your eyes have seen all that the Lord hath done against Beelphegor, how he hath destroyed all his worshippers from among you.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: But you that adhere to the Lord your God, are all alive until this present day.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: You know that I have taught you statutes and justices, as the Lord my God hath commanded me: so shall you do them in the land which you shall possess:

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And you shall observe, and fulfil them in practice. For this is your wisdom, and understanding in the sight of nations, that hearing all these precepts, they may say: Behold a wise and understanding people, a great nation.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Neither is there any other nation so great, that hath gods so nigh them, as our God is present to all our petitions.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: For what other nation is there so renowned that hath ceremonies, and just judgments, and all the law, which I will set forth this day before our eyes?

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Keep thyself therefore, and thy soul carefully. Forget not the words that thy eyes have seen, and let them not go out of thy heart all the days of thy life. Thou shalt teach them to thy sons and to thy grandsons,

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: From the day in which thou didst stand before the Lord thy God in Horeb, when the Lord spoke to me, saying: Call together the people unto me, that they may hear my words, and may learn to fear me all the time that they live on the earth, and may teach their children.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And you came to the foot of the mount, which burned even unto heaven: and there was darkness, and a cloud and obscurity in it.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And the Lord spoke to you from the midst of the fire. You heard the voice of his words, but you saw not any form at all.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And he shewed you his covenant, which he commanded you to do, and the ten words that he wrote in two tables of stone.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And he commanded me at that time that I should teach you the ceremonies and judgments which you shall do in the land, that you shall possess.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Keep therefore your souls carefully. You saw not any similitude in the day that the Lord God spoke to you in Horeb from the midst of the fire:

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Lest perhaps being deceived you might make you a graven similitude, or image of male or female,

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: The similitude of any beasts, that are upon the earth, or of birds, that fly under heaven,

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Or of creeping things, that move on the earth, or of fishes, that abide in the waters under the earth:

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Lest perhaps lifting up thy eyes to heaven, thou see the sun and the moon, and all the stars of heaven, and being deceived by error thou adore and serve them, which the Lord thy God created for the service of all the nations, that are under heaven.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: But the Lord hath taken you and brought you out of the iron furnaces of Egypt, to make you his people of inheritance, as it is this present day.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And the Lord was angry with me for your words, and he swore that I should not pass over the Jordan, nor enter into the excellent land, which he will give you.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Behold I die in this land, I shall not pass over the Jordan: you shall pass, and possess the goodly land.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Beware lest thou ever forget the covenant of the Lord thy God, which he hath made with thee: and make to thyself a graven likeness of those things which the Lord hath forbid to be made:

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Because the Lord thy God is a consuming fire, a jealous God.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: If you shall beget sons and grandsons, and abide in the land, and being deceived, make to yourselves any similitude, committing evil before the Lord your God, to provoke him to wrath:

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: I call this day heaven and earth to witness, that you shall quickly perish out of the land, which, when you have passed over the Jordan, you shall possess. You shall not dwell therein long, but the Lord will destroy you,

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And scatter you among all nations, and you shall remain a few among the nations, to which the Lord shall lead you.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And there you shall serve gods, that were framed with men’s hands: wood and stone, that neither see, nor hear, nor eat, nor smell.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And when thou shalt seek there the Lord thy God, thou shalt find him: yet so, if thou seek him with all thy heart, and all the affliction of thy soul.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: After all the things aforesaid shall find thee, in the latter time thou shalt return to the Lord thy God, and shalt hear his voice.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Because the Lord thy God is a merciful God: he will not leave thee, nor altogether destroy thee, nor forget the covenant, by which he swore to thy fathers.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Ask of the days of old, that have been before thy time from the day that God created man upon the earth, from one end of heaven to the other end thereof, if ever there was done the like thing, or it hath been known at any time,

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: That a people should hear the voice of God speaking out of the midst of fire, as thou hast heard, and lived:

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: If God ever did so as to go, and take to himself a nation out of the midst of nations by temptations, signs, and wonders, by fight, and a strong hand, and stretched out arm, and horrible visions according to all the things that the Lord your God did for you in Egypt, before thy eyes.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: That thou mightest know that the Lord he is God, and there is no other besides him.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: From heaven he made thee to hear his voice, that he might teach thee. And upon earth he shewed thee his exceeding great fire, and thou didst hear his words out of the midst of the fire,

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Because he loved thy fathers, and chose their seed after them. And he brought thee out of Egypt, going before thee with his great power,

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: To destroy at thy coming very great nations, and stronger than thou art, and to bring thee in, and give thee their land for a possession, as thou seest at this present day.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Know therefore this day, and think in thy heart that the Lord he is God in heaven above, and in the earth beneath, and there is no other.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Keep his precepts and commandments, which I command thee: that it may be well with thee, and thy children after thee, and thou mayst remain a long time upon the land, which the Lord thy God will give thee.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Then Moses set aside three cities beyond the Jordan at the east side,

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: That any one might flee to them who should kill his neighbour unwillingly, and was not his enemy a day or two before, and that he might escape to some one of these cities:

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Bosor in the wilderness, which is situate in the plains of the tribe of Ruben: and Ramoth in Galaad, which is in the tribe of Gad: and Golan in Basan, which is in the tribe of Manasses.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: This is the law, that Moses set before the children of Israel,

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And these are the testimonies and ceremonies and judgments, which he spoke to the children of Israel, when they came out of Egypt,

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Beyond the Jordan in the valley over against the temple of Phogor, in the land of Sehon king of the Amorrhites, that dwelt in Hesebon, whom Moses slew. And the children of Israel coming out of Egypt,

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Possessed his land, and the land of Og king of Basan, of the two kings of the Amorrhites, who were beyond the Jordan towards the rising of the sun:

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: From Aroer, which is situate upon the bank of the torrent Arnon, unto mount Sion, which is also called Hermon,

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: All the plain beyond the Jordan at the east side, unto the sea of the wilderness, and unto the foot of mount Phasga.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And Moses called all Israel, and said to them: Hear, O Israel, the ceremonies and judgments, which I speak in your ears this day: learn them, and fulfil them in work.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: The Lord our God made a covenant with us in Horeb.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: He made not the covenant with our fathers, but with us, who are now present and living.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: He spoke to us face to face in the mount out of the midst of fire.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: I was the mediator and stood between the Lord and you at that time, to shew you his words, for you feared the fire, and went not up into the mountain, and he said:

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: I am the Lord thy God, who brought thee out of the land of Egypt, out of the house of bondage.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Thou shalt not have strange gods in my sight.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Thou shalt not make to thy self a graven thing, nor the likeness of any things, that are in heaven above, or that are in the earth beneath, or that abide in the waters under the earth.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Thou shalt not adore them, and thou shalt not serve them. For I am the Lord thy God, a jealous God, visiting the iniquity of the fathers upon their children unto the third and fourth generation, to them that hate me,

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And shewing mercy unto many thousands, to them that love me, and keep my commandments.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Thou shalt not take the name of the Lord thy God in vain: for he shall not be unpunished that taketh his name upon a vain thing.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Observe the day of the sabbath, to sanctify it, as the Lord thy God hath commanded thee.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Six days shalt thou labour, and shalt do all thy works.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: The seventh is the day of the sabbath, that is, the rest of the Lord thy God. Thou shalt not do any work therein, thou nor thy son nor thy daughter, nor thy manservant nor thy maidservant, nor thy ox, nor thy ass, nor any of thy beasts, nor the stranger that is within thy gates: that thy manservant and thy maidservant may rest, even as thyself.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Remember that thou also didst serve in Egypt, and the Lord thy God brought thee out from thence with a strong hand, and a stretched out arm. Therefore hath he commanded thee that thou shouldst observe the sabbath day.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Honour thy father and mother, as the Lord thy God hath commanded thee, that thou mayst live a long time, and it may be well with thee in the land, which the Lord thy God will give thee.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Thou shalt not kill.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Neither shalt thou commit adultery.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And thou shalt not steal.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Neither shalt thou bear false witness against thy neighbour.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Thou shalt not covet thy neighbour’s wife: nor his house, nor his field, nor his manservant, nor his maidservant, nor his ox, nor his ass, nor any thing that is his.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: These words the Lord spoke to all the multitude of you in the mountain, out of the midst of the fire and the cloud, and the darkness, with a loud voice, adding nothing more: and he wrote them in two tables of stone, which he delivered unto me.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: But you, after you heard the voice out of the midst of the darkness, and saw the mountain burn, came to me, all the princes of the tribes and the elders, and you said:

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Behold the Lord our God hath shewn us his majesty and his greatness, we have heard his voice out of the midst of the fire, and have proved this day that God speaking with man, man hath lived.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Why shall we die therefore, and why shall this exceeding great fire comsume us: for if we hear the voice of the Lord our God any more, we shall die.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: What is all flesh, that it should hear the voice of the living God, who speaketh out of the midst of the fire, as we have heard, and be able to live?

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Approach thou rather: and hear all things that the Lord our God shall say to thee, and thou shalt speak to us, and we will hear and will do them.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And when the Lord had heard this, he said to me: I have heard the voice of the words of this people, which they spoke to thee: they have spoken all things well.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Who shall give them to have such a mind, to fear me, and to keep all my commandments at all times, that it may be well with them and with their children for ever?

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Go and say to them: Return into your tents.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: But stand thou here with me, and I will speak to thee all my commandments, and ceremonies and judgments: which thou shalt teach them, that they may do them in the land, which I will give them for a possession.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Keep therefore and do the things which the Lord God hath commanded you: you shall not go aside neither to the right hand, nor to the left.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: But you shall walk in the way that the Lord your God hath commanded, that you may live, and it may be well with you, and your days may be long in the land of your possession.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: These are the precepts, and ceremonies, and judgments, which the Lord your God commanded that I should teach you, and that you should do them in the land into which you pass over to possess it:

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: That thou mayst fear the Lord thy God, and keep all his commandments and precepts, which I command thee, and thy sons, and thy grandsons, all the days of thy life, that thy days may be prolonged.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Hear, O Israel, and observe to do the things which the Lord hath commanded thee, that it may be well with thee, and thou mayst be greatly multiplied, as the Lord the God of thy fathers hath promised thee a land flowing with milk and honey.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Hear, O Israel, the Lord our God is one Lord.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Thou shalt love the Lord thy God with thy whole heart, and with thy whole soul, and with thy whole strength.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And these words which I command thee this day, shall be in thy heart:

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And thou shalt tell them to thy children, and thou shalt meditate upon them sitting in thy house, and walking on thy journey, sleeping and rising.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And thou shalt bind them as a sign on thy hand, and they shall be and shall move between thy eyes.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And thou shalt write them in the entry, and on the doors of thy house.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And when the Lord thy God shall have brought thee into the land, for which he swore to thy fathers Abraham, Isaac, and Jacob: and shall have given thee great and goodly cities, which thou didst not build,

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Houses full of riches, which thou didst not set up, cisterns which thou didst not dig, vineyards and oliveyards, which thou didst not plant,

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And thou shalt have eaten and be full:

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Take heed diligently lest thou forget the Lord, who brought thee out of the land of Egypt, out of the house of bondage. Thou shalt fear the Lord thy God, and shalt serve him only, and thou shalt swear by his name.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: You shall not go after the strange gods of all the nations, that are round about you:

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Because the Lord thy God is a jealous God in the midst of thee: lest at any time the wrath of the Lord thy God be kindled against thee, and take thee away from the face of the earth.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Thou shalt not tempt the Lord thy God, as thou temptedst him in the place of temptation.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Keep the precepts of the Lord thy God, and the testimonies and ceremonies which he hath commanded thee.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And do that which is pleasing and good in the sight of the Lord, that it may be well with thee: and going in thou mayst possess the goodly land, concerning which the Lord swore to thy fathers,

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: That he would destroy all thy enemies before thee, as he hath spoken.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And when thy son shall ask thee to morrow, saying: What mean these testimonies, and ceremonies and judgments, which the Lord our God hath commanded us?

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Thou shalt say to him: We were bondmen of Pharao in Egypt, and the Lord brought us out of Egypt with a strong hand.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And he wrought signs and wonders great and very grievous in Egypt against Pharao, and all his house, in our sight,

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And he brought us out from thence, that he might bring us in and give us the land, concerning which he swore to our fathers.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And the Lord commanded that we should do all these ordinances, and should fear the Lord our God, that it might be well with us all the days of our life, as it is at this day.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And he will be merciful to us, if we keep and do all his precepts before the Lord our God, as he hath commanded us.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: When the Lord thy God shall have brought thee into the land, which thou art going in to possess, and shall have destroyed many nations before thee, the Hethite, and the Gergezite, and the Amorrhite, and the Chanaanite, and the Pherezite, and the Hevite, and the Jebusite, seven nations much more numerous than thou art, and stronger than thou:

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And the Lord thy God shall have delivered them to thee, thou shalt utterly destroy them. Thou shalt make no league with them, nor shew mercy to them:

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Neither shalt thou make marriages with them. Thou shalt not give thy daughter to his son, nor take his daughter for thy son:

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: For she will turn away thy son from following me, that he may rather serve strange gods, and the wrath of the Lord will be kindled, and will quickly destroy thee.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: But thus rather shall you deal with them: Destroy their altars, and break their statues, and cut down their groves, and burn their graven things.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Because thou art a holy people to the Lord thy God. The Lord thy God hath chosen thee, to be his peculiar people of all peoples that are upon the earth.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Not because you surpass all nations in number, is the Lord joined unto you, and hath chosen you, for you are the fewest of any people:

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: But because the Lord hath loved you, and hath kept his oath, which he swore to your fathers: and hath brought you out with a strong hand, and redeemed you from the house of bondage, out of the hand of Pharao the king of Egypt.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And thou shalt know that the Lord thy God, he is a strong and faithful God, keeping his covenant and mercy to them that love him, and to them that keep his commandments, unto a thousand generations:

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And repaying forthwith them that hate him, so as to destroy them, without further delay immediately rendering to them what they deserve.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Keep therefore the precepts and ceremonies and judgments, which I command thee this day to do.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: If after thou hast heard these judgments, thou keep and do them, the Lord thy God will also keep his covenant to thee, and the mercy which he swore to thy fathers:

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And he will love thee and multiply thee, and will bless the fruit of thy womb, and the fruit of thy land, thy corn, and thy vintage, thy oil, and thy herds, and the flocks of thy sheep upon the land, for which he swore to thy fathers that he would give it thee.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Blessed shalt thou be among all people. No one shall be barren among you of either sex, neither of men nor cattle.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: The Lord will take away from thee all sickness: and the grievous infirmities of Egypt, which thou knowest, he will not bring upon thee, but upon thy enemies.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Thou shalt consume all the people, which the Lord thy God will deliver to thee. Thy eye shall not spare them, neither shalt thou serve their gods, lest they be thy ruin.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: If thou say in thy heart: These nations are more than I, how shall I be able to destroy them?

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Fear not, but remember what the Lord thy God did to Pharao and to all the Egyptians,

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: The exceeding great plagues, which thy eyes saw, and the signs and wonders, and the strong hand, and the stretched out arm, with which the Lord thy God brought thee out: so will he do to all the people, whom thou fearest.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Moreover the Lord thy God will send also hornets among them, until he destroy and consume all that have escaped thee, and could hide themselves.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Thou shalt not fear them, because the Lord thy God is in the midst of thee, a God mighty and terrible:

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: He will consume these nations in thy sight by little and little and by degrees. Thou wilt not be able to destroy them altogether: lest perhaps the beasts of the earth should increase upon thee.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: But the Lord thy God shall deliver them in thy sight: and shall slay them until they be utterly destroyed.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And he shall deliver their kings into thy hands, and thou shalt destroy their names from under Heaven: no man shall be able to resist thee, until thou destroy them.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Their graven things thou shalt burn with fire: thou shalt not covet the silver and gold of which they are made, neither shalt thou take to thee any thing thereof, lest thou offend, because it is an abomination to the Lord thy God.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Neither shalt thou bring any thing of the idol into thy house, lest thou become an anathema, like it. Thou shalt detest it as dung, and shalt utterly abhor it as uncleanness and filth, because it is an anathema.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: All the commandments, that I command thee this day, take great care to observe: that you may live, and be multiplied, and going in may possess the land, for which the Lord swore to your fathers.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And thou shalt remember all the way through which the Lord thy God hath brought thee for forty years through the desert, to afflict thee and to prove thee, and that the things that were in thy heart might be made known, whether thou wouldst keep his commandments or no.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: He afflicted thee with want, and gave thee manna for thy food, which neither thou nor thy fathers knew: to shew that not in bread alone doth man live, but in every word that proceedeth from the mouth of God.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Thy raiment, with which thou wast covered, hath not decayed for age, and thy foot is not worn, lo this is the fortieth year,

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: That thou mayst consider in thy heart, that as a man traineth up his son, so the Lord thy God hath trained thee up.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: That thou shouldst keep the commandments of the Lord thy God, and walk in his ways, and fear him.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: For the Lord thy God will bring thee into a good land, of brooks and of waters, and of fountains: in the plains of which and the hills deep rivers break out:

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: A land of wheat, and barley, and vineyards, wherein fig trees and pomegranates, and oliveyards grow: a land of oil and honey.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Where without any want thou shalt eat thy bread, and enjoy abundance of all things: where the stones are iron, and out of its hills are dug mines of brass:

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: That when thou hast eaten, and art full, thou mayst bless the Lord thy God for the excellent land which he hath given thee.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Take heed, and beware lest at any time thou forget the Lord thy God, and neglect his commandments and judgments and ceremonies, which I command thee this day:

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Lest after thou hast eaten and art filled, hast built goodly houses, and dwelt in them,

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And shalt have herds of oxen and flocks of sheep, and plenty of gold and of silver, and of all things,

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Thy heart be lifted up, and thou remember not the Lord thy God, who brought thee out of the land of Egypt, out of the house of bondage:

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And was thy leader in the great and terrible wilderness, wherein there was the serpent burning with his breath, and the scorpion and the dipsas, and no waters at all: who brought forth streams out of the hardest rock,

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And fed thee in the wilderness with manna which thy fathers knew not. And after he had afflicted and proved thee, at the last he had mercy on thee,

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Lest thou shouldst say in thy heart: My own might, and the strength of my own hand have achieved all these things for me.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: But remember the Lord thy God, that he hath given thee strength, that he might fulfil his covenant, concerning which he swore to thy fathers, as this present day sheweth.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: But if thou forget the Lord thy God, and follow strange gods, and serve and adore them: behold now I foretell thee that thou shalt utterly perish.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: As the nations, which the Lord destroyed at thy entrance, so shall you also perish, if you be disobedient to the voice of the Lord your God.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Hear, O Israel: Thou shalt go over the Jordan this day; to possess nations very great, and stronger than thyself, cities great, and walled up to the sky,

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: A people great and tall, the sons of the Enacims, whom thou hast seen, and heard of, against whom no man is able to stand.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Thou shalt know therefore this day that the Lord thy God himself will pass over before thee, a devouring and consuming fire, to destroy and extirpate and bring them to nothing before thy face quickly, as he hath spoken to thee.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Say not in thy heart, when the Lord thy God shall have destroyed them in thy sight: For my justice hath the Lord brought me in to possess this land, whereas these nations are destroyed for their wickedness.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: For it is not for thy justices, and the uprightness of thy heart that thou shalt go in to possess their lands: but because they have done wickedly, they are destroyed at thy coming in: and that the Lord might accomplish his word, which he promised by oath to thy fathers Abraham, Isaac, and Jacob.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Know therefore that the Lord thy God giveth thee not this excellent land in possession for thy justices, for thou art a very stiffnecked people.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Remember, and forget not how thou provokedst the Lord thy God to wrath in the wilderness. From the day that thou camest out of Egypt unto this place, thou hast always strove against the Lord.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: For in Horeb, also thou didst provoke him, and he was angry, and would have destroyed thee,

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: When I went up into the mount to receive the tables of stone, the tables of the covenant which the Lord made with you: and I continued in the mount forty days and nights, neither eating bread, nor drinking water.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And the Lord gave me two tables of stone written with the finger of God, and containing all the words that he spoke to you in the mount from the midst of the fire, when the people were assembled together.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And when forty days were passed, and as many nights, the Lord gave me the two tables of stone, the tables of the covenant,

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And said to me: Arise, and go down from hence quickly: for thy people, which thou hast brought out of Egypt, have quickly forsaken the way that thou hast shewn them, and have made to themselves a molten idol.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And again the Lord said to me: I see that this people is stiffnecked:

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Let me alone that I may destroy them, and abolish their name from under heaven, and set thee over a nation, that is greater and stronger than this.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And when I came down from the burning mount, and held the two tables of the covenant with both hands,

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And saw that you had sinned against the Lord your God, and had made to yourselves a molten calf, and had quickly forsaken his way, which he had shewn you:

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: I cast the tables out of my hands, and broke them in your sight.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And I fell down before the Lord as before, forty days and nights neither eating bread, nor drinking water, for all your sins, which you had committed against the Lord, and had provoked him to wrath:

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: For I feared his indignation and anger, wherewith being moved against you, he would have destroyed you. And the Lord heard me this time also.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And he was exceeding angry against Aaron also, and would have destroyed him, and I prayed in like manner for him.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And your sin that you had committed, that is, the calf, I took, and burned it with fire, and breaking it into pieces, until it was as small as dust, I threw it into the torrent, which cometh down from the mountain.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: At the burning also, and at the place of temptation, and at the graves of lust you provoked the Lord:

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And when he sent you from Cadesbarne, saying: Go up, and possess the land that I have given you, and you slighted the commandment of the Lord your God, and did not believe him, neither would you hearken to his voice:

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: But were always rebellious from the day that I began to know you.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And I lay prostrate before the Lord forty days and nights, in which I humbly besought him, that he would not destroy you as he had threatened:

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And praying, I said: O Lord God, destroy not thy people, and thy inheritance, which thou hast redeemed in thy greatness, whom thou hast brought out of Egypt with a strong hand.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Remember thy servants Abraham, Isaac, and Jacob: look not on the stubbornness of this people, nor on their wickedness and sin:

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Lest perhaps the inhabitants of the land, out of which thou hast brought us, say: The Lord could not bring them into the land that he promised them, and he hated them: therefore he brought them out, that he might kill them in the wilderness,

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Who are thy people and thy inheritance, whom thou hast brought out by thy great strength, and in thy stretched out arm.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: At that time the Lord said to me: Hew thee two tables of stone like the former, and come up to me into the mount: and thou shalt make an ark of wood,

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And I will write on the tables the words that were in them, which thou brokest before, and thou shalt put them in the ark.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And I made an ark of setim wood. And when I had hewn two tables of stone like the former, I went up into the mount, having them in my hands.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And he wrote in the tables, according as he had written before, the ten words, which the Lord spoke to you in the mount from the midst of the fire, when the people were assembled: and he gave them to me.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And returning from the mount, I came down, and put the tables into the ark, that I had made, and they are there till this present, as the Lord commanded me.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And the children of Israel removed their camp from Beroth, of the children of Jacan into Mosera, where Aaron died and was buried, and Eleazar his son succeeded him in the priestly office.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: From thence they came to Gadgad, from which place they departed, and camped in Jetebatha, in a land of waters and torrents.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: At that time he separated the tribe of Levi, to carry the ark of the covenant of the Lord, and to stand before him in the ministry, and to bless in his name until this present day.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Wherefore Levi hath no part nor possession with his brethren: because the Lord himself is his possession, as the Lord thy God promised him.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And I stood in the mount, as before, forty days and nights: and the Lord heard me this time also, and would not destroy thee.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And he said to me: Go, and walk before the people, that they may enter, and possess the land, which I swore to their fathers that I would give them.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And now, Israel, what doth the Lord thy God require of thee, but that thou fear the Lord thy God, and walk in his ways, and love him, and serve the Lord thy God, with all thy heart, and with all thy soul:

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And keep the commandments of the Lord, and his ceremonies, which I command thee this day, that it may be well with thee?

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Behold heaven is the Lord’s thy God, and the heaven of heaven, the earth and all things that are therein.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And yet the Lord hath been closely joined to thy fathers, and loved them and chose their seed after them, that is to say, you, out of all nations, as this day it is proved.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Circumcise therefore the foreskin of your heart, and stiffen your neck no more.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Because the Lord your God he is the God of gods, and the Lord of lords, a great God and mighty and terrible, who accepteth no person nor taketh bribes.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: He doth judgment to the fatherless and the widow, loveth the stranger, and giveth him food and raiment.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And do you therefore love strangers, because you also were strangers in the land of Egypt.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Thou shalt fear the Lord thy God, and serve him only: to him thou shalt adhere, and shalt swear by his name.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: He is thy praise, and thy God, that hath done for thee these great and terrible things, which thy eyes have seen.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: In seventy souls thy fathers went down into Egypt: and behold now the Lord thy God hath multiplied thee as the stars of heaven.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Therefore love the Lord thy God and observe his precepts and ceremonies, his judgments and commandments at all times.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Know this day the things that your children know not, who saw not the chastisements of the Lord your God, his great doings and strong hand, and stretched out arm,

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: The signs and works which he did in the midst of Egypt to king Pharao, and to all his land,

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And to all the host of the Egyptians, and to their horses and chariots: how the waters of the Red Sea covered them, when they pursued you, and how the Lord destroyed them until this present day:

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And what he hath done to you in the wilderness, til you came to this place:

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And to Dathan and Abiron the sons of Eliab, who was the son of Ruben: whom the earth, opening her mouth swallowed up with their households and tents, and all their substance, which they had in the midst of Israel.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Your eyes have seen all the great works of the Lord, that he hath done,

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: That you may keep all his commandments, which I command you this day, and may go in, and possess the land, to which you are entering,

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And may live in it a long time: which the Lord promised by oath to your fathers, and to their seed, a land which floweth with milk and honey.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: For the land, which thou goest to possess, is not like the land of Egypt, from whence thou camest out, where, when the seed is sown, waters are brought in to water it after the manner of gardens.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: But it is a land of hills and plains, expecting rain from heaven.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And the Lord thy God doth always visit it, and his eyes are on it from the beginning of the year unto the end thereof.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: If then you obey my commandments, which I command you this day, that you love the Lord your God, and serve him with all your heart, and with all your soul:

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: He will give to your land the early rain and the latter rain, that you may gather in your corn, and your wine, and your oil,

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And your hay out of the fields to feed your cattle, and that you may eat and be filled.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Beware lest perhaps your heart be deceived, and you depart from the Lord, and serve strange gods, and adore them:

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And the Lord being angry shut up heaven, that the rain come not down, nor the earth yield her fruit, and you perish quickly from the excellent land, which the Lord will give you.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Lay up these words in your hearts and minds, and hang them for a sign on your hands, and place them between your eyes.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Teach your children that they meditate on them, when thou sittest in thy house, and when thou walkest on the way, and when thou liest down and risest up.
