source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Thou shalt write them upon the posts and the doors of thy house:

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: That thy days may be multiplied, and the days of thy children in the land which the Lord swore to thy fathers, that he would give them as long as the heaven hangeth over the earth.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: For if you keep the commandments which I command you, and do them, to love the Lord your God, and walk in all his ways, cleaving unto him,

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: The Lord will destroy all these nations before your face, and you shall possess them, which are greater and stronger than you.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Every place, that your foot shall tread upon, shall be yours. From the desert, and from Libanus, from the great river Euphrates unto the western sea shall be your borders.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: None shall stand against you: the Lord your God shall lay the dread and fear of you upon all the land that you shall tread upon, as he hath spoken to you.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Behold I set forth in your sight this day a blessing and a curse:

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: A blessing, if you obey the commandments of the Lord your God, which I command you this day:

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: A curse, if you obey not the commandments of the Lord your God, but revolt from the way which now I shew you, and walk after strange gods which you know not.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And when the Lord thy God shall have brought thee into the land, whither thou goest to dwell, thou shalt put the blessing upon mount Garizim, the curse upon mount Hebal:

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Which are beyond the Jordan, behind the way that goeth to the setting of the sun, in the land of the Chanaanite who dwelleth in the plain country over against Galgala, which is near the valley that reacheth and entereth far.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: For you shall pass over the Jordan, to possess the land, which the Lord your God will give you, that you may have it and possess it.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: See therefore that you fulfil the ceremonies and judgments, which I shall set this day before you.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: These are the precepts and judgments, that you must do in the land, which the Lord the God of thy fathers will give thee, to possess it all the days that thou shalt walk upon the earth.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Destroy all the places in which the nations, that you shall possess, worshipped their gods upon high mountains, and hills, and under every shady tree:

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Overthrow their altars, and break down their statues, burn their groves with fire, and break their idols in pieces: destroy their names out of those places.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: You shall not do so to the Lord your God:

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: But you shall come to the place, which the Lord your God shall choose out of all your tribes, to put his name there, and to dwell in it:

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And you shall offer in that place your holocausts and victims, the tithes and firstfruits of your hands and your vows and gifts, the firstborn of your herds and your sheep.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And you shall eat there in the sight of the Lord your God: and you shall rejoice in all things, whereunto you shall put your hand, you and your houses wherein the Lord your God hath blessed you.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: You shall not do there the things we do here this day, every man that which seemeth good to himself.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: For until this present time you are not come to rest, and to the possession, which the Lord your God will give you.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: You shall pass over the Jordan, and shall dwell in the land which the Lord your God will give you, that you may have rest from all enemies round about: and may dwell without any fear,

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: In the place, which the Lord your God shall choose, that his name may be therein. Thither shall you bring all the things that I command you, holocausts, and victims, and tithes, and the firstfruits of your hands: and whatsoever is the choicest in the gifts which you shall vow to the Lord.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: There shall you feast before the Lord your God, you and your sons and your daughters, your menservants and maidservants, and the Levite that dwelleth in your cities. For he hath no other part and possession among you.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Beware lest thou offer thy holocausts in every place that thou shalt see:

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: But in the place which the Lord shall choose in one of thy tribes shalt thou offer sacrifices, and shalt do all that I command thee.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: But if thou desirest to eat, and the eating of flesh delight thee, kill, and eat according to the blessing of the Lord thy God, which he hath given thee, in thy cities: whether it be unclean, that is to say, having blemish or defect: or clean, that is to say, sound and without blemish, such as may be offered, as the roe, and the hart, shalt thou eat it:

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Only the blood thou shalt not eat, but thou shalt pour it out upon the earth as water.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Thou mayst not eat in thy towns the tithes of thy corn, and thy wine, and thy oil, the firstborn of thy herds and thy cattle, nor any thing that thou vowest, and that thou wilt offer voluntarily, and the firstfruits of thy hands:

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: But thou shalt eat them before the Lord thy God in the place which the Lord thy God shall choose, thou and thy son and thy daughter, and thy manservant, and maidservant, and the Levite that dwelleth in thy cities: and thou shalt rejoice and be refreshed before the Lord thy God in all things, whereunto thou shalt put thy hand.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Take heed thou forsake not the Levite all the time that thou livest in the land.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: When the Lord thy God shall have enlarged thy borders, as he hath spoken to thee, and thou wilt eat the flesh that thy soul desireth:

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And if the place which the Lord thy God shall choose, that his name should be there, be far off, thou shalt kill of thy herds and of thy flocks, as I have commanded thee, and shalt eat in thy towns, as it pleaseth thee.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Even as the roe and the hart is eaten, so shalt thou eat them: both the clean and unclean shall eat of them alike.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Only beware of this, that thou eat not the blood, for the blood is for the soul: and therefore thou must not eat the soul with the flesh:

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: But thou shalt pour it upon the earth as water,

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: That it may be well with thee and thy children after thee, when thou shalt do that which is pleasing in the sight of the Lord.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: But the things which thou hast sanctified and vowed to the Lord, thou shalt take, and shalt come to the place which the Lord shall choose:

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And shalt offer thy oblations, the flesh and the blood upon the altar of the Lord thy God: the blood of thy victims thou shalt pour on the altar: and the flesh thou thyself shalt eat.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Observe and hear all the things that I command thee, that it may be well with thee and thy children after thee for ever, when thou shalt do what is good and pleasing in the sight of the Lord thy God.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: When the Lord thy God shall have destroyed before thy face the nations, which thou shalt go in to possess, and when thou shalt possess them, and dwell in their land:

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Beware lest thou imitate them, after they are destroyed at thy coming in, and lest thou seek after their ceremonies, saying: As these nations have worshipped their gods, so will I also worship.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Thou shalt not do in like manner to the Lord thy God. For they have done to their gods all the abominations which the Lord abhorreth, offering their sons and daughters, and burning them with fire.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: What I command thee, that only do thou to the Lord: neither add any thing, nor diminish.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: If there rise in the midst of thee a prophet or one that saith he hath dreamed a dream, and he foretell a sign and a wonder,

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And that come to pass which he spoke, and he say to thee: Let us go and follow strange gods, which thou knowest not, and let us serve them:

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Thou shalt not hear the words of that prophet or dreamer: for the Lord your God trieth you, that it may appear whether you love him with all your heart, and with all your soul, or not.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Follow the Lord your God, and fear him, and keep his commandments, and hear his voice: him you shall serve, and to him you shall cleave.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And that prophet or forger of dreams shall be slain: because he spoke to draw you away from the Lord your God, who brought you out of the land of Egypt, and redeemed you from the house of bondage: to make thee go out of the way, which the Lord thy God commanded thee: and thou shalt take away the evil out of the midst of thee.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: If thy brother the son of thy mother, or thy son, or daughter, or thy wife that is in thy bosom, or thy friend, whom thou lovest as thy own soul, would persuade thee secretly, saying: Let us go, and serve strange gods, which thou knowest not, nor thy fathers,

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Of all the nations round about, that are near or afar off, from one end of the earth to the other,

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Consent not to him, hear him not, neither let thy eye spare him to pity and conceal him,

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: But thou shalt presently put him to death. Let thy hand be first upon him, and afterwards the hands of all the people.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: With stones shall he be stoned to death: because he would have withdrawn thee from the Lord thy God, who brought thee out of the land of Egypt, from the house of bondage:

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: That all Israel hearing may fear, and may do no more any thing like this.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: If in one of thy cities, which the Lord thy God shall give thee to dwell in, thou hear some say:

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Children of Belial are gone out of the midst of thee, and have withdrawn the inhabitants of their city, and have said: Let us go, and serve strange gods which you know not:

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Inquire carefully and diligently, the truth of the thing by looking well into it, and if thou find that which is said to be certain, and that this abomination hath been really committed,

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Thou shalt forthwith kill the inhabitants of that city with the edge of the sword, and shalt destroy it and all things that are in it, even the cattle.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And all the household goods that are there, thou shalt gather together in the midst of the streets thereof, and shall burn them with the city itself, so as to comsume all for the Lord thy God, and that it be a heap for ever: it shall be built no more.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And there shall nothing of that anathema stick to thy hand: that the Lord may turn from the wrath of his fury, and may have mercy on thee, and multiply thee as he swore to thy fathers,

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: When thou shalt hear the voice of the Lord thy God, keeping all his precepts, which I command thee this day, that thou mayst do what is pleasing in the sight of the Lord thy God.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Be ye children of the Lord your God: you shall not cut yourselves, nor make any baldness for the dead;

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Because thou art a holy people to the Lord thy God: and he chose thee to be his peculiar people of all nations that are upon the earth.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Eat not the things that are unclean.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: These are the beasts that you shall eat, the ox, and the sheep, and the goat,

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: The hart and the roe, the buffle, the chamois, the pygarg, the wild goat, the camelopardalus.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Every beast that divideth the hoof in two parts, and cheweth the cud, you shall eat.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: But of them that chew the cud, but divide not the hoof, you shall not eat, such as the camel, the hare, and the cherogril: because they chew the cud, but divide not the hoof, they shall be unclean to you.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: The swine also, because it divideth the hoof, but cheweth not the cud, shall be unclean, their flesh you shall not eat, and their carcasses you shall not touch.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: These shall you eat of all that abide in the waters: All that have fins and scales, you shall eat.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Such as are without fins and scales, you shall not eat, because they are unclean.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: All birds that are clean you shall eat.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: The unclean eat not: to wit, the eagle, and the grype, and the osprey,

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: The ringtail, and the vulture, and the kite according to their kind:

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And all of the raven’s kind:

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And the ostrich, and the owl, and the larus, and the hawk according to its kind:

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: The heron, and the swan, and the stork,

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And the cormorant, the porphirion, and the night crow,

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: The bittern, and the charadrion, every one in their kind: the houp also and the bat.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Every thing that creepeth, and hath little wings, shall be unclean, and shall not be eaten.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: All that is clean, you shall eat.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: But whatsoever is dead of itself, eat not thereof. Give it to the stranger, that is within thy gates, to eat, or sell it to him: because thou art the holy people of the Lord thy God. Thou shalt not boil a kid in the milk of his dam.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Every year thou shalt set aside the tithes of all thy fruits that the earth bringeth forth,

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And thou shalt eat before the Lord thy God in the place which he shall choose, that his name may be called upon therein, the tithe of thy corn, and thy wine, and thy oil, and the firstborn of thy herds and thy sheep: that thou mayst learn to fear the Lord thy God at all times.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: But when the way and the place which the Lord thy God shall choose, are far off, and he hath blessed thee, and thou canst not carry all these things thither,

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Thou shalt sell them all, and turn them into money, and shalt carry it in thy hand, and shalt go to the place which the Lord shall choose:

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And thou shalt buy with the same money whatsoever pleaseth thee, either of the herds or of sheep, wine also and strong drink, and all that thy soul desireth: and thou shalt eat before the Lord thy God, and shalt feast, thou and thy house:

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And the Levite that is within thy gates, beware thou forsake him not, because he hath no other part in thy possession.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: The third year thou shalt separate another tithe of all things that grow to thee at that time, and shalt lay it up within thy gates.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And the Levite that hath no other part nor possession with thee, and the stranger and the fatherless and the widow, that are within thy gates, shall come and shall eat and be filled: that the Lord thy God may bless thee in all the works of thy hands that thou shalt do.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: In the seventh year thou shalt make a remission,

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Which shall be celebrated in this order. He to whom any thing is owing from his friend or neighbour or brother, cannot demand it again, because it is the year of remission of the Lord.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Of the foreigner or stranger thou mayst exact it: of thy countryman and neighbour thou shalt not have power to demand it again.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And there shall be no poor nor beggar among you: that the Lord thy God may bless thee in the land which he will give thee in possession.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Yet so if thou hear the voice of the Lord thy God, and keep all things that he hath ordained, and which I command thee this day, he will bless thee, as he hath promised.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Thou shalt lend to many nations, and thou shalt borrow of no man. Thou shalt have dominion over very many nations, and no one shall have dominion over thee.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: If one of thy brethren that dwelleth within thy gates of thy city in the land which the Lord thy God will give thee, come to poverty: thou shalt not harden thy heart, nor close thy hand,

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: But shalt open it to the poor man, thou shalt lend him, that which thou perceivest he hath need of.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Beware lest perhaps a wicked thought steal in upon thee, and thou say in thy heart: The seventh year of remission draweth nigh; and thou turn away thy eyes from thy poor brother, denying to lend him that which he asketh: lest he cry against thee to the Lord, and it become a sin unto thee.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: But thou shalt give to him: neither shalt thou do any thing craftily in relieving his necessities: that the Lord thy God may bless thee at all times, and in all things to which thou shalt put thy hand.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: There will not be wanting poor in the land of thy habitation: therefore I command thee to open thy hand to thy needy and poor brother, that liveth in the land.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: When thy brother a Hebrew man, or Hebrew woman is sold to thee, and hath served thee six years, in the seventh year thou shalt let him go free:

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And when thou sendest him out free, thou shalt not let him go away empty:

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: But shall give him for his way out of thy flocks, and out of thy barnfloor, and thy winepress, wherewith the Lord thy God shall bless thee.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Remember that thou also wast a bondservant in the land of Egypt, and the Lord thy God made thee free, and therefore I now command thee this.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: But if he say: I will not depart: because he loveth thee, and thy house, and findeth that he is well with thee:

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Thou shalt take an awl, and bore through his ear in the door of thy house, and he shall serve thee for ever: thou shalt do in like manner to thy womanservant also.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Turn not away thy eyes from them when thou makest them free: because he hath served thee six years according to the wages of a hireling: that the Lord thy God may bless thee in all the works that thou dost.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Of the firstlings, that come of thy herds and thy sheep, thou shalt sanctify to the Lord thy God whatsoever is of the male sex. Thou shalt not work with the firstling of a bullock, and thou shalt not shear the firstlings of thy sheep.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: In the sight of the Lord thy God shalt thou eat them every year, in the place that the Lord shall choose, thou and thy house.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: But if it have a blemish, or be lame, or blind, or in any part disfigured or feeble, it shall not be sacrificed to the Lord thy God.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: But thou shalt eat it within the gates of thy city: the clean and the unclean shall eat them alike, as the roe and as the hart.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Only thou shalt take heed not to eat their blood, but pour it out on the earth as water.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Observe the month of new corn, which is the first of the spring, that thou mayst celebrate the phase to the Lord thy God: because in this month the Lord thy God brought thee out of Egypt by night.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And thou shalt sacrifice the phase to the Lord thy God, of sheep, and of oxen, in the place which the Lord thy God shall choose, that his name may dwell there.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Thou shalt not eat with it leavened bread: seven days shalt thou eat without leaven, the bread of affliction, because thou camest out of Egypt in fear: that thou mayst remember the day of thy coming out of Egypt, all the days of thy life.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: No leaven shall be seen in all thy coasts for seven days, neither shall any of the flesh of that which was sacrificed the first day in the evening remain until morning.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Thou mayst not immolate the phase in any one of thy cities, which the Lord thy God will give thee:

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: But in the place which the Lord thy God shall choose, that his name may dwell there: thou shalt immolate the phase in the evening, at the going down of the sun, at which time thou camest out of Egypt.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And thou shalt dress, and eat it in the place which the Lord thy God shall choose, and in the morning rising up thou shalt go into thy dwellings.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Six days shalt thou eat unleavened bread: and on the seventh day, because it is the assembly of the Lord thy God, thou shalt do no work.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Thou shalt number unto thee seven weeks from that day, wherein thou didst put the sickle to the corn.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And thou shalt celebrate the festival of weeks to the Lord thy God, a voluntary oblation of thy hand, which thou shalt offer according to the blessing of the Lord thy God.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And thou shalt feast before the Lord thy God, thou, and thy son, and thy daughter, and thy manservant, and thy maidservant, and the Levite that is within thy gates, and the stranger and the fatherless, and the widow, who abide with you: in the place which the Lord thy God shall choose, that his name may dwell there:

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And thou shalt remember that thou wast a servant in Egypt: and thou shalt keep and do the things that are commanded.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Thou shalt celebrate the solemnity also of tabernacles seven days, when thou hast gathered in thy fruit of the barnfloor and of the winepress.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And thou shalt make merry in thy festival time, thou, thy son, and thy daughter, thy manservant, and thy maidservant, the Levite also and the stranger, and the fatherless and the widow that are within thy gates.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Seven days shalt thou celebrate feasts to the Lord thy God in the place which the Lord shall choose: and the Lord thy God will bless thee in all thy fruits, and in every work of thy hands, and thou shalt be in joy.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Three times in a year shall all thy males appear before the Lord thy God in the place which he shall choose: in the feast of unleavened bread, in the feast of weeks, and in the feast of tabernacles. No one shall appear with his hands empty before the Lord:

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: But every one shall offer according to what he hath, according to the blessing of the Lord his God, which he shall give him.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Thou shalt appoint judges and magistrates in all thy gates, which the Lord thy God shall give thee, in all thy tribes: that they may judge the people with just judgment,

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And not go aside to either part. Thou shalt not accept person nor gifts: for gifts blind the eyes of the wise, and change the words of the just.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Thou shalt follow justly after that which is just: that thou mayst live and possess the land, which the Lord thy God shall give thee.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Thou shalt plant no grove, nor any tree near the altar of the Lord thy God:

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Neither shalt thou make nor set up to thyself a statue: which things the Lord thy God hateth.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Thou shalt not sacrifice to the Lord thy God a sheep, or an ox, wherein there is blemish, or any fault: for that is an abomination to the Lord thy God.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: When there shall be found among you within any of thy gates, which the Lord thy God shall give thee, man or woman that do evil in the sight of the Lord thy God, and transgress his covenant,

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: So as to go and serve strange gods, and adore them, the sun and the moon, and all the host of heaven, which I have not commanded:

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And this is told thee, and hearing it thou hast inquired diligently, and found it to be true, and that the abomination is committed in Israel:

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Thou shalt bring forth the man or the woman, who have committed that most wicked thing, to the gates of thy city, and they shall be stoned.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: By the mouth of two or three witnesses shall he die that is to be slain. Let no man be put to death, when only one beareth witness against him.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: The hands of the witnesses shall be first upon him to kill him, and afterwards the hands of the rest of the people: that thou mayst take away the evil out of the midst of thee.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: If thou perceive that there be among you a hard and doubtful matter in judgment between blood and blood, cause and cause, leprosy and leprosy: and thou see that the words of the judges within thy gates do vary: arise, and go up to the place, which the Lord thy God shall choose.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And thou shalt come to the priests of the Levitical race, and to the judge, that shall be at that time: and thou shalt ask of them, and they shall shew thee the truth of the judgment.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And thou shalt do whatsoever they shall say, that preside in the place, which the Lord shall choose, and what they shall teach thee,

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: According to his law; and thou shalt follow their sentence: neither shalt thou decline to the right hand nor to the left hand.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: But he that will be proud, and refuse to obey the commandment of the priest, who ministereth at that time to the Lord thy God, and the decree of the judge, that man shall die, and thou shalt take away the evil from Israel:

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And all the people hearing it shall fear, that no one afterwards swell with pride.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: When thou art come into the land, which the Lord thy God will give thee, and possessest it, and shalt say: I will set a king over me, as all nations have that are round about:

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Thou shalt set him whom the Lord thy God shall choose out of the number of thy brethren. Thou mayst not make a man of another nation king, that is not thy brother.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And when he is made king, he shall not multiply horses to himself, nor lead back the people into Egypt, being lifted up with the number of his horsemen, especially since the Lord hath commanded you to return no more the same way.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: He shall not have many wives, that may allure his mind, nor immense sums of silver and gold.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: But after he is raised to the throne of his kingdom, he shall copy out to himself the Deuteronomy of this law in a volume, taking the copy of the priests of the Levitical tribe,

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And he shall have it with him, and shall read it all the days of his life, that he may learn to fear the Lord his God, and keep his words and ceremonies, that are commanded in the law;

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And that his heart be not lifted up with pride over his brethren, nor decline to the right or to the left, that he and his sons may reign a long time over Israel.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: The priests and Levites, and all that are of the same tribe, shall have no part nor inheritance with the rest of Israel, because they shall eat the sacrifices of the Lord, and his oblations,

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And they shall receive nothing else of the possession of their brethren: for the Lord himself is their inheritance, as he hath said to them.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: This shall be the priest’s due from the people, and from them that offer victims: whether they sacrifice an ox, or a sheep, they shall give to the priest the shoulder and the breast:

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: The firstfruits also of corn, of wine, and of oil, and a part of the wool from the shearing of their sheep.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: For the Lord thy God hath chosen him of all thy tribes, to stand and to minister to the name of the Lord, him and his sons for ever.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: If a Levite go out of any one of the cities throughout all Israel, in which he dwelleth, and have a longing mind to come to the place which the Lord shall choose,

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: He shall minister in the name of the Lord his God, as all his brethren the Levites do, that shall stand at that time before the Lord.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: He shall receive the same portion of food that the rest do: besides that which is due to him in his own city, by succession from his fathers.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: When thou art come into the land which the Lord thy God shall give thee, beware lest thou have a mind to imitate the abominations of those nations.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Neither let there be found among you any one that shall expiate his son or daughter, making them to pass through the fire: or that consulteth soothsayers, or observeth dreams and omens, neither let there be any wizard,

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Nor charmer, nor any one that consulteth pythonic spirits, or fortune tellers, or that seeketh the truth from the dead.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: For the Lord abhorreth all these things, and for these abominations he will destroy them at thy coming.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Thou shalt be perfect, and without spot before the Lord thy God.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: These nations, whose land thou shalt possess, hearken to soothsayers and diviners: but thou art otherwise instructed by the Lord thy God.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: The Lord thy God will raise up to thee a PROPHET of thy nation and of thy brethren like unto me: him thou shalt hear:

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: As thou desiredst of the Lord thy God in Horeb, when the assembly was gathered together, and saidst: Let me not hear any more the voice of the Lord my God, neither let me see any more this exceeding great fire, lest I die.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And the Lord said to me: They have spoken all things well.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: I will raise them up a prophet out of the midst of their brethren like to thee: and I will put my words in his mouth, and he shall speak to them all that I shall command him.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And he that will not hear his words, which he shall speak in my name, I will be the revenger.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: But the prophet, who being corrupted with pride, shall speak in my name things that I did not command him to say, or in the name of strange gods, shall be slain.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And if in silent thought thou answer: How shall I know the word that the Lord hath not spoken?

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Thou shalt have this sign: Whatsoever that same prophet foretelleth in the name of the Lord, and it cometh not to pass: that thing the Lord hath not spoken, but the prophet hath forged it by the pride of his mind: and therefore thou shalt not fear him.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: When the Lord thy God hath destroyed the nations, whose land he will deliver to thee, and thou shalt possess it, and shalt dwell in the cities and houses thereof:

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Thou shalt separate to thee three cities in the midst of the land, which the Lord will give thee in possession,

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Paving diligently the way: and thou shalt divide the whole province of thy land equally into three parts: that he who is forced to flee for manslaughter, may have near at hand whither to escape.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: This shall be the law of the slayer that fleeth, whose life is to be saved: He that killeth his neighbor ignorantly, and who is proved to have had no hatred against him yesterday and the day before:

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: But to have gone with him to the wood to hew wood, and in cutting down the tree the axe slipped out of his hand, and the iron slipping from the handle struck his friend, and killed him: he shall flee to one of the cities aforesaid, and live:

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Lest perhaps the next kinsman of him whose blood was shed, pushed on by his grief should pursue, and apprehend him, if the way be too long, and take away the life of him who is not guilty of death, because he is proved to have had no hatred before against him that was slain.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Therefore I command thee, that thou separate three cities at equal distance one from another.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And when the Lord thy God shall have enlarged thy borders, as he swore to thy fathers, and shall give thee all the land that he promised them,

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: (Yet so, if thou keep his commandments, and do the things which I command thee this day, that thou love the Lord thy God, and walk in his ways at all times) thou shalt add to thee other three cities, and shalt double the number of the three cities aforesaid:

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: That innocent blood may not be shed in the midst of the land which the Lord thy God will give thee to possess, lest thou be guilty of blood.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: But if any man hating his neighbour, lie in wait for his life, and rise and strike him, and he die, and he flee to one of the cities aforesaid,

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: The ancients of his city shall send, and take him out of the place of refuge, and shall deliver him into the hand of the kinsman of him whose blood was shed, and he shall die.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Thou shalt not pity him, and thou shalt take away the guilt of innocent blood out of Israel, that it may be well with thee.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Thou shalt not take nor remove thy neighbour’s landmark, which thy predecessors have set in thy possession, which the Lord thy God will give thee in the land that thou shalt receive to possess.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: One witness shall not rise up against any man, whatsoever the sin or wickedness be: but in the mouth of two or three witnesses every word shall stand.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: If a lying witness stand against a man, accusing him of transgression,

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Both of them, between whom the controversy is, shall stand before the Lord in the sight of the priests and the judges that shall be in those days.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And when after most diligent inquisition, they shall find that the false witness hath told a lie against his brother:

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: They shall render to him as he meant to do to his brother, and thou shalt take away the evil out of the midst of thee:

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: That others hearing may fear, and may not dare to do such things.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Thou shalt not pity him, but shalt require life for life, eye for eye, tooth for tooth, hand for hand, foot for foot.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: If thou go out to war against thy enemies, and see horsemen and chariots, and the numbers of the enemy’s army greater than thine, thou shalt not fear them: because the Lord thy God is with thee, who brought thee out of the land of Egypt.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And when the battle is now at hand, the priest shall stand before the army, and shall speak to the people in this manner:

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Hear, O Israel, you join battle this day against your enemies, let not your heart be dismayed, be not afraid, do not give back, fear ye them not:

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Because the Lord your God is in the midst of you, and will fight for you against your enemies, to deliver you from danger.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And the captains shall proclaim through every band in the hearing of the army: What man is there, that hath built a new house, and hath not dedicated it? let him go and return to his house, lest he die in the battle, and another man dedicate it.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: What man is there, that hath planted a vineyard, and hath not as yet made it to be common, whereof all men may eat? let him go, and return to his house, lest he die in the battle, and another man execute his office.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: What man is there, that hath espoused a wife, and not taken her? let him go, and return to his house, lest he die in the war, and another man take her.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: After these things are declared they shall add the rest, and shall speak to the people: What man is there that is fearful, and faint hearted? let him go, and return to his house, lest he make the hearts of his brethren to fear, as he himself is possessed with fear.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And when the captains of the army shall hold their peace, and have made an end of speaking, every man shall prepare their bands to fight.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: If at any time thou come to fight against a city, thou shalt first offer it peace.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: If they receive it, and open the gates to thee, all the people that are therein, shall be saved, and shall serve thee paying tribute.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: But if they will not make peace, and shall begin war against thee, thou shalt besiege it.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And when the Lord thy God shall deliver it into thy hands, thou shalt slay all that are therein of the male sex, with the edge of the sword,

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Excepting women and children, cattle and other things, that are in the city. And thou shalt divide all the prey to the army, and thou shalt eat the spoils of thy enemies, which the Lord thy God shall give thee.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: So shalt thou do to all cities that are at a great distance from thee, and are not of these cities which thou shalt receive in possession.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: But of those cities that shall be given thee, thou shalt suffer none at all to live:

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: But shalt kill them with the edge of the sword, to wit, the Hethite, and the Amorrhite, and the Chanaanite, the Pherezite, and the Hevite, and the Jebusite, as the Lord thy God hath commanded thee:

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Lest they teach you to do all the abominations which they have done to their gods: and you should sin against the Lord your God.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: When thou hast besieged a city a long time, and hath compassed it with bulwarks, to take it, thou shalt not cut down the trees that may be eaten of, neither shalt thou spoil the country round about with axes: for it is a tree, and not a man, neither can it increase the number of them that fight against thee.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: But if there be any trees that are not fruitful, but wild, and fit for other uses, cut them down, and make engines, until thou take the city, which fighteth against thee.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: When there shall be found in the land, which the Lord thy God will give thee, the corpse of a man slain, and it is not known who is guilty of the murder,

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Thy ancients and judges shall go out, and shall measure from the place where the body lieth the distance of every city round about:

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And the ancients of that city which they shall perceive to be nearer than the rest, shall take a heifer of the herd, that hath not drawn in the yoke, nor ploughed the ground,

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And they shall bring her into a rough and stony valley, that never was ploughed, nor sown: and there they shall strike off the head of the heifer:

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And the priests the sons of Levi shall come, whom the Lord thy God hath chosen to minister to him, and to bless in his name, and that by their word every matter should be decided, and whatsoever is clean or unclean should be judged.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And the ancients of that city shall come to the person slain, and shall wash their hands over the heifer that was killed in the valley,

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And shall say: Our hands did not shed this blood, nor did our eyes see it.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Be merciful to thy people Israel, whom thou hast redeemed, O Lord, and lay not innocent blood to their charge, in the midst of thy people Israel. And the guilt of blood shall be taken from them:

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And thou shalt be free from the innocent’s blood, that was shed, when thou shalt have done what the Lord hath commanded thee.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: If thou go out to fight against thy enemies, and the Lord thy God deliver them into thy hand, and thou lead them away captives,

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And seest in the number of the captives a beautiful woman, and lovest her, and wilt have her to wife,

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Thou shalt bring her into thy house: and she shall shave her hair, and pare her nails,

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And shall put off the raiment, wherein she was taken: and shall remain in thy house, and mourn for her father and mother one month: and after that thou shalt go in unto her, and shalt sleep with her, and she shall be thy wife.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: But if afterwards she please thee not, thou shalt let her go free, but thou mayst not sell her for money nor oppress her by might because thou hast humbled her.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: If a man have two wives, one beloved, and the other hated, and they have had children by him, and the son of the hated be the firstborn,

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And he meaneth to divide his substance among his sons: he may not make the son of the beloved the firstborn, and prefer him before the son of the hated.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: But he shall acknowledge the son of the hated for the firstborn, and shall give him a double portion of all he hath: for this is the first of his children, and to him are due the first birthrights.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: If a man have a stubborn and unruly son, who will not hear the commandments of his father or mother, and being corrected, slighteth obedience:

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: They shall take him and bring him to the ancients of the city, and to the gate of judgment,

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And shall say to them: This our son is rebellious and stubborn, he slighteth hearing our admonitions, he giveth himself to revelling, and to debauchery and banquetings:

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: The people of the city shall stone him: and he shall die, that you may take away the evil out of the midst of you, and all Israel hearing it may be afraid.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: When a man hath committed a crime for which he is to be punished with death, and being condemned to die is hanged on a gibbet:

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: His body shall not remain upon the tree, but shall be buried the same day: for he is accursed of God that hangeth on a tree: and thou shalt not defile thy land, which the Lord thy God shall give thee in possession.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Thou shalt not pass by if thou seest thy brother’s ox, or his sheep go astray: but thou shalt bring them back to thy brother.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And if thy brother be not nigh, or thou know him not: thou shalt bring them to thy house, and they shall be with thee until thy brother seek them, and receive them.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Thou shalt do in like manner with his ass, and with his raiment, and with every thing that is thy brother’s, which is lost: if thou find it, neglect it not as pertaining to another.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: If thou see thy brother’s ass or his ox to be fallen down in the way, thou shalt not slight it, but shalt lift it up with him.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: A woman shall not be clothed with man’s apparel, neither shall a man use woman’s apparel: for he that doth these things is abominable before God.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: If thou find as thou walkest by the way, a bird’s nest in a tree, or on the ground, and the dam sitting upon the young or upon the eggs: thou shalt not take her with her young:

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: But shalt let her go, keeping the young which thou hast caught: that it may be well with thee, and thou mayst live a long time.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: When thou buildest a new house, thou shalt make a battlement to the roof round about: lest blood be shed in thy house, and thou be guilty, if any one slip, and fall down headlong.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Thou shalt not sow thy vineyard with divers seeds: lest both the seed which thou hast sown, and the fruit of the vineyard, be sanctified together.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Thou shalt not plough with an ox and an ass together.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Thou shalt not wear a garment that is woven of woollen and linen together.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Thou shalt make strings in the hem at the four corners of thy cloak, wherewith thou shalt be covered.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: If a man marry a wife, and afterwards hate her,

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And seek occasions to put her away, laying to her charge a very ill name, and say: I took this woman to wife, and going in to her, I found her not a virgin:

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Her father and mother shall take her, and shall bring with them the tokens of her virginity to the ancients of the city that are in the gate:

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And the father shall say: I gave my daughter unto this man to wife: and because he hateth her,

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: He layeth to her charge a very ill name, so as to say: I found not thy daughter a virgin: and behold these are the tokens of my daughter’s virginity. And they shall spread the cloth before the ancients of the city:

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And the ancients of that city shall take that man, and beat him,

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Condemning him besides in a hundred sicles of silver, which he shall give to the damsel’s father, because he hath defamed by a very ill name a virgin of Israel: and he shall have her to wife, and may not put her away all the days of his life.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: But if what he charged her with be true, and virginity be not found in the damsel:

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: They shall cast her out of the doors of her father’s house, and the men of the city shall stone her to death, and she shall die: because she hath done a wicked thing in Israel, to play the whore in her father’s house: and thou shalt take away the evil out of the midst of thee.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: If a man lie with another man’s wife, they shall both die, that is to say, the adulterer and the adulteress: and thou shalt take away the evil out of Israel.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: If a man have espoused a damsel that is a virgin, and some one find her in the city, and lie with her,

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Thou shalt bring them both out to the gate of that city, and they shall be stoned: the damsel, because she cried not out, being in the city: the man, because he hath humbled his neighbour’s wife. And thou shalt take away the evil from the midst of thee.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: But if a man find a damsel that is betrothed, in the field, and taking hold of her, lie with her, he alone shall die:

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: The damsel shall suffer nothing, neither is she guilty of death: for as a robber riseth against his brother, and taketh away his life, so also did the damsel suffer:

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: She was alone in the field: she cried, and there was no man to help her.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: If a man find a damsel that is a virgin, who is not espoused, and taking her, lie with her, and the matter come to judgment:

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: He that lay with her shall give to the father of the maid fifty sicles of silver, and shall have her to wife, because he hath humbled her: he may not put her away all the days of his life.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: No man shall take his father’s wife, nor remove his covering.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: An eunuch, whose testicles are broken or cut away, or yard cut off, shall not enter into the church of the Lord.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: A mamzer, that is to say, one born of a prostitute, shall not enter into the church of the Lord, until the tenth generation.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: The Ammonite and the Moabite, even after the tenth generation shall not enter into the church of the Lord for ever:

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Because they would not meet you with bread and water in the way, when you came out of Egypt: and because they hired against thee Balaam, the son of Beor, from Mesopotamia in Syria, to curse thee.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And the Lord thy God would not hear Balaam, and he turned his cursing into thy blessing, because he loved thee.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Thou shalt not make peace with them, neither shalt thou seek their prosperity all the days of thy life for ever.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Thou shalt not abhor the Edomite, because he is thy brother: nor the Egyptian, because thou wast a stranger in his land.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: They that are born of them, in the third generation shall enter into the church of the Lord.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: When thou goest out to war against thy enemies, thou shalt keep thyself from every evil thing.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: If there be among you any man, that is defiled in a dream by night, he shall go forth out of the camp,

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And shall not return, before he be washed with water in the evening: and after sunset he shall return into the camp.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Thou shalt have a place without the camp, to which thou mayst go for the necessities of nature,

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Carrying a paddle at thy girdle. And when thou sittest down, thou shalt dig round about, and with the earth that is dug up thou shalt cover

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: That which thou art eased of: (for the Lord thy God walketh in the midst of thy camp, to deliver thee, and to give up thy enemies to thee:) and let thy camp be holy, and let no uncleanness appear therein, lest he go away from thee.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Thou shalt not deliver to his master the servant that is fled to thee.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: He shall dwell with thee in the place that shall please him, and shall rest in one of thy cities: give him no trouble.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: There shall be no whore among the daughters of Israel, nor whoremonger among the sons of Israel.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Thou shalt not offer the hire of a strumpet, nor the price of a dog, in the house of the Lord thy God, whatsoever it be that thou hast vowed: because both these are an abomination to the Lord thy God.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Thou shalt not lend to thy brother money to usury, nor corn, nor any other thing:

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: But to the stranger. To thy brother thou shalt lend that which he wanteth, without usury: that the Lord thy God may bless thee in all thy works in the land, which thou shalt go in to possess.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: When thou hast made a vow to the Lord thy God, thou shalt not delay to pay it: because the Lord thy God will require it. And if thou delay, it shall be imputed to thee for a sin.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: If thou wilt not promise, thou shalt be without sin.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: But that which is once gone out of thy lips, thou shalt observe, and shalt do as thou hast promised to the Lord thy God, and hast spoken with thy own will and with thy own mouth.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Going into thy neighbour’s vineyard, thou mayst eat as many grapes as thou pleasest: but must carry none out with thee:

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: If thou go into thy friend’s corn, thou mayst break the ears, and rub them in thy hand: but not reap them with a sickle.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: If a man take a wife, and have her, and she find not favour in his eyes, for some uncleanness: he shall write a bill of divorce, and shall give it in her hand, and send her out of his house.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And when she is departed, and marrieth another husband,

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And he also hateth her, and hath given her a bill of divorce, and hath sent her out of his house or is dead:

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: The former husband cannot take her again to wife: because she is defiled, and is become abominable before the Lord: lest thou cause thy land to sin, which the Lord thy God shall give thee to possess.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: When a man hath lately taken a wife, he shall not go out to war, neither shall any public business be enjoined him, but he shall be free at home without fault, that for one year he may rejoice with his wife.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Thou shalt not take the nether, nor the upper millstone to pledge: for he hath pledged his life to thee.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: If any man be found soliciting his brother of the children of Israel, and selling him shall take a price, he shall be put to death, and thou shalt take away the evil from the midst of thee.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Observe diligently that thou incur not the stroke of the leprosy, but thou shalt do whatsoever the priests of the Levitical race shall teach thee, according to what I have commanded them, and fulfil thou it carefully.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Remember what the Lord your God did to Mary, in the way when you came out of Egypt.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: When thou shalt demand of thy neighbour any thing that he oweth thee, thou shalt not go into his house to take away a pledge:

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: But thou shalt stand without, and he shall bring out to thee what he hath.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: But if he be poor, the pledge shall not lodge with thee that night,

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: But thou shalt restore it to him presently before the going down of the sun: that he may sleep in his own raiment and bless thee, and thou mayst have justice before the Lord thy God.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Thou shalt not refuse the hire of the needy, and the poor, whether he be thy brother, or a stranger that dwelleth with thee in the land, and is within thy gates:

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: But thou shalt pay him the price of his labour the same day, before the going down of the sun, because he is poor, and with it maintaineth his life: lest he cry against thee to the Lord, and it be reputed to thee for a sin.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: The fathers shall not be put to death for the children, nor the children for the fathers, but every one shall die for his own sin,

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Thou shalt not pervert the judgment of the stranger nor of the fatherless, neither shalt thou take away the widow’s raiment for a pledge.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Remember that thou wast a slave in Egypt, and the Lord thy God delivered thee from thence. Therefore I command thee to do this thing.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: When thou hast reaped the corn in thy field, and hast forgot and left a sheaf, thou shalt not return to take it away: but thou shalt suffer the stranger, and the fatherless and the widow to take it away: that the Lord thy God may bless thee in all the works of thy hands.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: If thou have gathered the fruit of thy olive trees, thou shalt not return to gather whatsoever remaineth on the trees: but shalt leave it for the stranger, for the fatherless, and the widow.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: If thou make the vintage of thy vineyard, thou shalt not gather the clusters that remain, but they shall be for the stranger, the fatherless, and the widow.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Remember that thou also wast a bondman in Egypt, and therefore I command thee to do this thing.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: If there be a controversy between men, and they call upon the judges: they shall give the prize of justice to him whom they perceive to be just: and him whom they find to be wicked, they shall condemn of wickedness.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And if they see that the offender be worthy of stripes: they shall lay him down, and shall cause him to be beaten before them. According to the measure of the sin shall the measure also of the stripes be:

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Yet so, that they exceed not the number of forty: lest thy brother depart shamefully torn before thy eyes.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Thou shalt not muzzle the ox that treadeth out thy corn on the floor.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: When brethren dwell together, and one of them dieth without children, the wife of the deceased shall not marry to another: but his brother shall take her, and raise up seed for his brother:

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And the first son he shall have of her he shall call by his name, that his name be not abolished out of Israel.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner
