text: But if he will not take his brother’s wife, who by law belongeth to him, the woman shall go to the gate of the city, and call upon the ancients, and say: My husband’s brother refuseth to raise up his brother’s name in Israel: and will not take me to wife.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And they shall cause him to be sent for forthwith, and shall ask him. If he answer: I will not take her to wife:

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: The woman shall come to him before the ancients, and shall take off his shoe from his foot, and spit in his face, and say: So shall it be done to the man that will not build up his brother’s house:

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And his name shall be called in Israel, the house of the unshod.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: If two men have words together, and one begin to fight against the other, and the other’s wife willing to deliver her husband out of the hand of the stronger, shall put forth her hand, and take him by the secrets,

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Thou shalt cut off her hand, neither shalt thou be moved with any pity in her regard.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Thou shalt not have divers weights in thy bag, a greater and a less:

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Neither shall there be in thy house a greater bushel and a less.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Thou shalt have a just and a true weight, and thy bushel shall be equal and true: that thou mayest live a long time upon the land which the Lord thy God shall give thee.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: For the Lord thy God abhorreth him that doth these things, and he hateth all injustice.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Remember what Amalec did to thee in the way when thou camest out of Egypt:

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: How he met thee: and slew the hindmost of the army, who sat down, being weary, when thou wast spent with hunger and labour, and he feared not God.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Therefore when the Lord thy God shall give thee rest, and shall have subdued all the nations round about in the land which he hath promised thee: thou shalt blot out his name from under heaven. See thou forget it not.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And when thou art come into the land which the Lord thy God will give thee to possess, and hast conquered it, and dwellest in it:

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Thou shalt take the first of all thy fruits, and put them in a basket, and shalt go to the place which the Lord thy God shall choose, that his name may be invocated there:

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And thou shalt go to the priest that shall be in those days, and say to him: I profess this day before the Lord thy God, that I am come into the land, for which he swore to our fathers, that he would give it us.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And the priest taking the basket at thy hand, shall set it before the altar of the Lord thy God:

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And thou shalt speak thus in the sight of the Lord thy God: The Syrian pursued my father, who went down into Egypt, and sojourned there in a very small number, and grew into a nation great and strong and of an infinite multitude.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And the Egyptians afflicted us, and persecuted us, laying on us most grievous burdens:

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And we cried to the Lord God of our fathers: who heard us, and looked down upon our affliction, and labour, and distress:

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And brought us out of Egypt with a strong hand, and a stretched out arm, with great terror, with signs and wonders:

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And brought us into this place, and gave us this land flowing with milk and honey.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And therefore now I offer the firstfruits of the land which the Lord hath given me. And thou shalt leave them in the sight of the Lord thy God, adoring the Lord thy God.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And thou shalt feast in all the good things which the Lord thy God hath given thee, and thy house, thou and the Levite, and the stranger that is with thee.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: When thou hast made an end of tithing all thy fruits, in the third year of tithes thou shalt give it to the Levite, and to the stranger, and to the fatherless, and to the widow, that they may eat within thy gates, and be filled:

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And thou shalt speak thus in the sight of the Lord thy God: I have taken that which was sanctified out of my house, and I have given it to the Levite, and to the stranger, and to the fatherless, and to the widow, as thou hast commanded me: I have not transgressed thy commandments nor forgotten thy precepts.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: I have not eaten of them in my mourning, nor separated them for any uncleanness, nor spent any thing of them in funerals. I have obeyed the voice of the Lord my God, and have done all things as thou hast commanded me.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Look from thy sanctuary, and thy high habitation of heaven, and bless thy people Israel, and the land which thou hast given us, as thou didst swear to our fathers, a land flowing with milk and honey.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: This day the Lord thy God hath commanded thee to do these commandments and judgments: and to keep and fulfil them with all thy heart, and with all thy soul.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Thou hast chosen the Lord this day to be thy God, and to walk in his ways and keep his ceremonies, and precepts, and judgments, and obey his command.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And the Lord hath chosen thee this day, to be his peculiar people, as he hath spoken to thee, and to keep all his commandments:

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And to make thee higher than all nations which he hath created, to his own praise, and name, and glory: that thou mayst be a holy people of the Lord thy God, as he hath spoken.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And Moses with the ancients of Israel commanded the people, saying: Keep every commandment that I command you this day.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And when you are passed over the Jordan into the land which the Lord thy God will give thee, thou shalt set up great stones, and shalt plaster them over with plaster,

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: That thou mayst write on them all the words of this law, when thou art passed over the Jordan: that thou mayst enter into the land which the Lord thy God will give thee, a land flowing with milk and honey, as he swore to thy fathers.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Therefore when you are passed over the Jordan, set up the stones which I command you this day, in mount Hebal, and thou shalt plaster them with plaster:

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And thou shalt build there an altar to the Lord thy God, of stones which iron hath not touched,

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And of stones not fashioned nor polished: and thou shalt offer upon it holocausts to the Lord thy God:

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And shalt immolate peace victims, and eat there, and feast before the Lord thy God.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And thou shalt write upon the stones all the words of this law plainly and clearly.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And Moses and the priests of the race of Levi said to all Israel: Attend, and hear, O Israel: This day thou art made the people of the Lord thy God:

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Thou shalt hear his voice, and do the commandments and justices which I command thee.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And Moses commanded the people in that day, saying:

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: These shall stand upon mount Garizim to bless the people, when you are passed the Jordan: Simeon, Levi, Juda, Issachar, Joseph, and Benjamin.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And over against them shall stand on mount Hebal to curse: Ruben, Gad, and Aser, and Zabulon, Dan, and Nephtali.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And the Levites shall pronounce, and say to all the men of Israel with a loud voice:

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Cursed be the man that maketh a graven and molten thing, the abomination of the Lord, the work of the hands of artificers, and shall put it in a secret place: and all the people shall answer and say: Amen.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Cursed be he that honoureth not his father and mother: and all the people shall say: Amen.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Cursed be he that removeth his neighbour’s landmarks: and all the people shall say: Amen.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Cursed be he that maketh the blind to wander out of his way: and all the people shall say: Amen.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Cursed be he that perverteth the judgment of the stranger, of the fatherless and the widow: and all the people shall say: Amen.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Cursed be he that lieth with his father’s wife, and uncovereth his bed: and all the people shall say: Amen.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Cursed be he that lieth with any beast: and all the people shall say: Amen.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Cursed be he that lieth with his sister, the daughter of his father, or of his mother: and all the people shall say: Amen.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Cursed be he that lieth with his mother-in-law: and all the people shall say: Amen.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Cursed be he that secretly killeth his neighbour: and all the people shall say: Amen.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Cursed be he that taketh gifts, to slay an innocent person: and all the people shall say: Amen.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Cursed be he that abideth not in the words of this law, and fulfilleth them not in work: and all the people shall say: Amen.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Now if thou wilt hear the voice of the Lord thy God, to do and keep all his commandments, which I command thee this day, the Lord thy God will make thee higher than all the nations that are on the earth.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And all these blessings shall come upon thee and overtake thee: yet so if thou hear his precepts.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Blessed shalt thou be in the city, and blessed in the field.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Blessed shall be the fruit of thy womb, and the fruit of thy ground, and the fruit of thy cattle, the droves of thy herds, and the folds of thy sheep.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Blessed shall be thy barns and blessed thy stores.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Blessed shalt thou be coming in and going out.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: The Lord shall cause thy enemies, that rise up against thee, to fall down before thy face: one way shall they come out against thee, and seven ways shall they flee before thee.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: The Lord will send forth a blessing upon thy storehouses, and upon all the works of thy hands: and will bless thee in the land that thou shalt receive.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: The Lord will raise thee up to be a holy people to himself, as he swore to thee: if thou keep the commandments of the Lord thy God, and walk in his ways.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And all the people of the earth shall see that the name of the Lord is invocated upon thee, and they shall fear thee.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: The Lord will make thee abound with all goods, with the fruit of thy womb, and the fruit of thy cattle, with the fruit of thy land, which the Lord swore to thy fathers that he would give thee.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: The Lord will open his excellent treasure, the heaven, that it may give rain in due season: and he will bless all the works of thy hands. And thou shalt lend to many nations, and shalt not borrow of any one.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And the Lord shall make thee the head and not the tail: and thou shalt be always above, and not beneath: yet so if thou wilt hear the commandments of the Lord thy God which I command thee this day, and keep and do them,

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And turn not away from them neither to the right hand, nor to the left, nor follow strange gods, nor worship them.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: But if thou wilt not hear the voice of the Lord thy God, to keep and to do all his commandments and ceremonies, which I command thee this day, all these curses shall come upon thee, and overtake thee.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Cursed shalt thou be in the city, cursed in the field.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Cursed shall be thy barn, and cursed thy stores.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Cursed shall be the fruit of thy womb, and the fruit of thy ground, the herds of thy oxen, and the flocks of thy sheep.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Cursed shalt thou be coming in, and cursed going out.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: The Lord shall send upon thee famine and hunger, and a rebuke upon all the works which thou shalt do: until he consume and destroy thee quickly, for thy most wicked inventions, by which thou hast forsaken me.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: May the Lord set the pestilence upon thee, until he consume thee out of the land, which thou shalt go in to possess.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: May the Lord afflict thee with miserable want, with the fever and with cold, with burning and with heat, and with corrupted air and with blasting, and pursue thee till thou perish.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Be the heaven, that is over thee, of brass: and the ground thou treadest on, of iron.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: The Lord give thee dust for rain upon thy land, and let ashes come down from heaven upon thee, till thou be consumed.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: The Lord make thee to fall down before thy enemies, one way mayst thou go out against them, and flee seven ways, and be scattered throughout all the kingdoms of the earth.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And be thy carcass meat for all the fowls of the air, and the beasts of the earth, and be there none to drive them away.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: The Lord strike thee with the ulcer of Egypt, and the part of thy body, by which the dung is cast out, with the scab and with the itch: so that thou canst not be healed.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: The Lord strike thee with madness and blindness and fury of mind.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And mayst thou grope at midday as the blind is wont to grope in the dark, and not make straight thy ways. And mayst thou at all times suffer wrong, and be oppressed with violence, and mayst thou have no one to deliver thee.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Mayst thou take a wife, and another sleep with her. Mayst thou build a house, and not dwell therein. Mayest thou plant a vineyard and not gather the vintage thereof.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: May thy ox be slain before thee, and thou not eat thereof. May thy ass be taken away in thy sight, and not restored to thee. May thy sheep be given to thy enemies, and may there be none to help thee.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: May thy sons and thy daughters be given to another people, thy eyes looking on, and languishing at the sight of them all the day, and may there be no strength in thy hand.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: May a people which thou knowest not, eat the fruits of thy land, and all thy labours: and mayst thou always suffer oppression, and be crushed at all times.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And be astonished at the terror of those things which thy eyes shall see:

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: May the Lord strike thee with a very sore ulcer in the knees and in the legs, and be thou incurable from the sole of the foot to the top of the head.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: The Lord shall bring thee, and thy king, whom thou shalt have appointed over thee, into a nation which thou and thy fathers know not: and there thou shalt serve strange gods, wood and stone.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And thou shalt be lost, as a proverb and a byword to all people, among whom the Lord shall bring thee in.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Thou shalt cast much seed into the ground, and gather little: because the locusts shall consume all.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Thou shalt plant a vineyard, and dig it, and shalt not drink the wine, nor gather any thing thereof: because it shall be wasted with worms.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Thou shalt have olive trees in all thy borders, and shalt not be anointed with the oil: for the olives shall fall off and perish.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Thou shalt beget sons and daughters, and shalt not enjoy them: because they shall be led into captivity.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: The blast shall consume all the trees and the fruits of thy ground.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: The stranger that liveth with thee in the land, shall rise up over thee, and shall be higher: and thou shalt go down, and be lower.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: He shall lend to thee, and thou shalt not lend to him. He shall be as the head, and thou shalt be the tail.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And all these curses shall come upon thee, and shall pursue and overtake thee, till thou perish: because thou heardst not the voice of the Lord thy God, and didst not keep his commandments and ceremonies which he commanded thee.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And they shall be as signs and wonders on thee, and on thy seed for ever.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Because thou didst not serve the Lord thy God with joy and gladness of heart, for the abundance of all things:

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Thou shalt serve thy enemy, whom the Lord will send upon thee, in hunger, and thirst, and nakedness, and in want of all things: and he shall put an iron yoke upon thy neck, till he consume thee.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: The Lord will bring upon thee a nation from afar, and from the uttermost ends of the earth, like an eagle that flyeth swiftly, whose tongue thou canst not understand,

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: A most insolent nation, that will shew no regard to the ancients, nor have pity on the infant,

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And will devour the fruit of thy cattle, and the fruits of thy land: until thou be destroyed, and will leave thee no wheat, nor wine, nor oil, nor herds of oxen, nor flocks of sheep: until he destroy thee.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And consume thee in all thy cities, and thy strong and high wall be brought down, wherein thou trustedst in all thy land. Thou shalt be besieged within thy gates in all thy land which the Lord thy God will give thee:

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And thou shalt eat the fruit of thy womb, and the flesh of thy sons and of thy daughters, which the Lord thy God shall give thee, in the distress and extremity wherewith thy enemy shall oppress thee.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: The man that is nice among you, and very delicate, shall envy his own brother, and his wife, that lieth in his bosom,

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: So that he will not give them of the flesh of his children, which he shall eat: because he hath nothing else in the siege and the want, wherewith thy enemies shall distress thee within all thy gates.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: The tender and delicate woman, that could not go upon the ground, nor set down her foot for over much niceness and tenderness, will envy her husband who lieth in her bosom, the flesh of her son, and of her daughter,

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And the filth of the afterbirths, that come forth from between her thighs, and the children that are born the same hour. For they shall eat them secretly for the want of all things, in the siege and distress, wherewith thy enemy shall oppress thee within thy gates.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: If thou wilt not keep, and fulfil all the words of this law, that are written in this volume, and fear his glorious and terrible name: that is, The Lord thy God:

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: The Lord shall increase thy plagues, and the plagues of thy seed, plagues great and lasting, infirmities grievous and perpetual.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And he shall bring back on thee all the afflictions of Egypt, which thou wast afraid of, and they shall stick fast to thee.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Moreover the Lord will bring upon thee all the diseases, and plagues, that are not written in the volume of this law till he consume thee:

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And you shall remain few in number, who before were as the stars of heaven for multitude, because thou heardst not the voice of the Lord thy God.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And as the Lord rejoiced upon you before doing good to you, and multiplying you: so he shall rejoice destroying and bringing you to nought, so that you shall be taken away from the land which thou shalt go in to possess.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: The Lord shall scatter thee among all people, from the farthest parts of the earth to the ends thereof: and there thou shalt serve strange gods, which both thou art ignorant of and thy fathers, wood and stone.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Neither shalt thou be quiet, even in those nations, nor shall there be any rest for the sole of thy foot. For the Lord will give thee a fearful heart, and languishing eyes, and a soul consumed with pensiveness:

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And thy life shall be as it were hanging before thee. Thou shalt fear night and day, neither shalt thou trust thy life.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: In the morning thou shalt say: Who will grant me evening? and at evening: Who will grant me morning? for the fearfulness of thy heart, wherewith thou shalt be terrified, and for those things which thou shalt see with thy eyes.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: The Lord shall bring thee again with ships into Egypt, by the way whereof he said to thee that thou shouldst see it no more. There shalt thou be set to sale to thy enemies for bondmen and bondwomen, and no man shall buy you.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: These are the words of the covenant which the Lord commanded Moses to make with the children of Israel in the land of Moab: beside that covenant which he made with them in Horeb.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And Moses called all Israel, and said to them: You have seen all the things that the Lord did before you in the land of Egypt to Pharao, and to all his servants, and to his whole land.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: The great temptations, which thy eyes have seen, those mighty signs and wonders,

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And the Lord hath not given you a heart to understand, and eyes to see, and ears that may hear, unto this present day.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: He hath brought you forty years through the desert: your garments are not worn out, neither are the shoes of your feet consumed with age.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: You have not eaten bread, nor have you drunk wine or strong drink: that you might know that I am the Lord your God.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And you came to this place: and Sehon king of Hesebon, and Og king of Basan, came out against us to fight. And we slew them.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And took their land, and delivered it for a possession to Ruben and Gad, and the half tribe of Manasses.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Keep therefore the words of this covenant, and fulfil them: that you may understand all that you do.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: You all stand this day before the Lord your God, your princes, and tribes, and ancients, and doctors, all the people of Israel,

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Your children and your wives, and the stranger that abideth with thee in the camp, besides the hewers of wood, and them that bring water:

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: That thou mayst pass in the covenant of the Lord thy God, and in the oath which this day the Lord thy God maketh with thee.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: That he may raise thee up a people to himself, and he may be thy God as he hath spoken to thee, and as he swore to thy fathers Abraham, Isaac, and Jacob.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Neither with you only do I make this covenant, and confirm these oaths,

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: But with all that are present and that are absent.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: For you know how we dwelt in the land of Egypt, and how we have passed through the midst of nations, and passing through them,

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: You have seen their abominations and filth, that is to say, their idols, wood and stone, silver and gold, which they worshipped.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Lest perhaps there should be among you a man or a woman, a family or a tribe, whose heart is turned away this day from the Lord our God, to go and serve the gods of those nations: and there should be among you a root bringing forth gall and bitterness.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And when he shall hear the words of this oath, he should bless himself in his heart saying: I shall have peace, and will walk on in the naughtiness of my heart: and the drunken may consume the thirsty,

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And the Lord should not forgive him: but his wrath and jealousy against that man should be exceedingly enkindled at that time, and all the curses that are written in this volume should light upon him: and the Lord should blot out his name from under heaven,

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And utterly destroy him out of all the tribes of Israel, according to the curses that are contained in the book of this law and covenant:

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And the following generation shall say, and the children that shall be born hereafter, and the strangers that shall come from afar, seeing the plagues of that land and the evils wherewith the Lord hath afflicted it,

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Burning it with brimstone, and the heat of salt, so that it cannot be sown any more, nor any green thing grow therein, after the example of the destruction of Sodom and Gomorrha, Adama and Seboim, which the Lord destroyed in his wrath and indignation:

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And all the nations shall say: Why hath the Lord done thus to this land? what meaneth this exceeding great heat of his wrath?

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And they shall answer: Because they forsook the covenant of the Lord, which he made with their fathers, when he brought them out of the land of Egypt:

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And they have served strange gods, and adored them, whom they knew not, and for whom they had not been assigned:

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Therefore the wrath of the Lord was kindled against this land, to bring upon it all the curses that are written in this volume:

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And he hath cast them out of their land, in anger and in wrath, and in very great indignation, and hath thrown them into a strange land, as it is seen this day.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Secret things to the Lord our God: things that are manifest, to us and to our children for ever, that we may do all the words of this law.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Now when all these things shall be come upon thee, the blessing or the curse, which I have set forth before thee, and thou shalt be touched with repentance of thy heart among all the nations, into which the Lord thy God shall have scattered thee,

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And shalt return to him, and obey his commandments, as I command thee this day, thou and thy children, with all thy heart, and with all thy soul:

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: The Lord thy God will bring back again thy captivity, and will have mercy on thee, and gather thee again out of all the nations, into which he scattered thee before.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: If thou be driven as far as the poles of heaven, the Lord thy God will fetch thee back from hence,

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And will take thee to himself, and bring thee into the land which thy fathers possessed, and thou shalt possess it: and blessing thee, he will make thee more numerous than were thy fathers.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: The Lord thy God will circumcise thy heart, and the heart of thy seed: that thou mayst love the Lord thy God with all thy heart and with all thy soul, that thou mayst live.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And he will turn all these curses upon thy enemies, and upon them that hate and persecute thee.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: But thou shalt return, and hear the voice of the Lord thy God, and shalt do all the commandments which I command thee this day:

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And the Lord thy God will make thee abound in all the works of thy hands, in the fruit of thy womb, and in the fruit of thy cattle, in the fruitfulness of thy land, and in the plenty of all things. For the Lord will return to rejoice over thee in all good things, as he rejoiced in thy fathers:

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Yet so if thou hear the voice of the Lord thy God, and keep his precepts and ceremonies, which are written in this law: and return to the Lord thy God with all thy heart, and with all thy soul.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: This commandment, that I command thee this day is not above thee, nor far off from thee:

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Nor is it in heaven, that thou shouldst say: Which of us can go up to heaven to bring it unto us, and we may hear and fulfil it in work?

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Nor is it beyond the sea: that thou mayst excuse thyself, and say: Which of us can cross the sea, and bring it unto us: that we may hear, and do that which is commanded?

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: But the word is very nigh unto thee, in thy mouth and in thy heart, that thou mayst do it.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Consider that I have set before thee this day life and good, and on the other hand death and evil:

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: That thou mayst love the Lord thy God, and walk in his ways, and keep his commandments and ceremonies and judgments, and thou mayst live, and he may multiply thee, and bless thee in the land, which thou shalt go in to possess.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: But if thy heart be turned away, so that thou wilt not hear, and being deceived with error thou adore strange gods, and serve them:

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: I foretell thee this day that thou shalt perish, and shalt remain but a short time in the land, to which thou shalt pass over the Jordan, and shalt go in to possess it.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: I call heaven and earth to witness this day, that I have set before you life and death, blessing and cursing. Choose therefore life, that both thou and thy seed may live:

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And that thou mayst love the Lord thy God, and obey his voice, and adhere to him (for he is thy life, and the length of thy days,) that thou mayst dwell in the land, for which the Lord swore to thy fathers Abraham, Isaac, and Jacob that he would give it them.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And Moses went, and spoke all these words to all Israel,

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And he said to them: I am this day a hundred and twenty years old, I can no longer go out and come in, especially as the Lord also hath said to me: Thou shalt not pass over this Jordan.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: The Lord thy God then will pass over before thee: he will destroy all these nations in thy sight, and thou shalt possess them: and this Josue shall go over before thee, as the Lord hath spoken.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And the Lord shall do to them as he did to Sehon and Og the kings of the Amorrhites, and to their land, and shall destroy them.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Therefore when the Lord shall have delivered these also to you, you shall do in like manner to them as I have commanded you.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Do manfully and be of good heart: fear not, nor be ye dismayed at their sight: for the Lord thy God he himself is thy leader, and will not leave thee nor forsake thee.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And Moses called Josue, and said to him before all Israel: Take courage, and be valiant: for thou shalt bring this people into the land which the Lord swore he would give to their fathers, and thou shalt divide it by lot.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And the Lord who is your leader, he himself will be with thee: he will not leave thee, nor forsake thee: fear not, neither be dismayed.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And Moses wrote this law, and delivered it to the priests the sons of Levi, who carried the ark of the covenant of the Lord, and to all the ancients of Israel.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And he commanded them, saying: After seven years, in the year of remission, in the feast of tabernacles,

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: When all Israel come together, to appear in the sight of the Lord thy God in the place which the Lord shall choose, thou shalt read the words of this law before all Israel, in their hearing.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And the people being all assembled together, both men and women, children and strangers, that are within thy gates: that hearing they may learn, and fear the Lord your God, and keep, and fulfil all the words of this law:

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: That their children also, who now are ignorant, may hear, and fear the Lord their God, all the days that they live in the land whither you are going over the Jordan to possess it.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And the Lord said to Moses: Behold the days of thy death are nigh: call Josue, and stand ye in the tabernacle of the testimony, that I may give him a charge. So Moses and Josue went and stood in the tabernacle of the testimony:

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And the Lord appeared there in the pillar of a cloud, which stood in the entry of the tabernacle.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And the Lord said to Moses: Behold thou shalt sleep with thy fathers, and this people rising up will go a fornicating after strange gods in the land, to which it goeth in to dwell: there will they forsake me, and will make void the covenant, which I have made with them,

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And my wrath shall be kindled against them in that day: and I will forsake them, and will hide my face from them, and they shall be devoured: all evils and afflictions shall find them, so that they shall say in that day: In truth it is because God is not with me, that these evils have found me.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: But I will hide, and cover my face in that day, for all the evils which they have done, because they have followed strange gods.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Now therefore write you this canticle, and teach the children of Israel: that they may know it by heart, and sing it by mouth, and this song may be unto me for a testimony among the children of Israel.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: For I will bring them into the land, for which I swore to their fathers, that floweth with milk and honey. And when they have eaten, and are full and fat, they will turn away after strange gods, and will serve them: and will despise me, and make void my covenant.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And after many evils and afflictions shall have come upon them, this canticle shall answer them for a testimony, which no oblivion shall take away out of the mouth of their seed. For I know their thoughts, and what they are about to do this day, before that I bring them into the land which I have promised them.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Moses therefore wrote the canticle, and taught it to the children of Israel.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And the Lord commanded Josue the son of Nun, and said: Take courage, and be valiant: for thou shalt bring the children of Israel into the land which I have promised, and I will be with thee.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Therefore after Moses had wrote the words of this law in a volume, and finished it:

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: He commanded the Levites, who carried the ark of the covenant of the Lord, saying:

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Take this book, and put it in the side of the ark of the covenant of the Lord your God: that it may be there for a testimony against thee.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: For I know thy obstinacy, and thy most stiff neck. While I am yet living, and going in with you, you have always been rebellious against the Lord: how much more when I shall be dead?

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Gather unto me all the ancients of your tribes, and your doctors, and I will speak these words in their hearing, and will call heaven and earth to witness against them.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: For I know that, after my death, you will do wickedly, and will quickly turn aside from the way that I have commanded you: and evils shall come upon you in the latter times, when you shall do evil in the sight of the Lord, to provoke him by the works of your hands.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Moses therefore spoke, in the hearing of the whole assembly of Israel, the words of this canticle, and finished it even to the end.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Hear, O ye heavens, the things I speak, let the earth give ear to the words of my mouth.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Let my doctrine gather as the rain, let my speech distil as the dew, as a shower upon the herb, and as drops upon the grass.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Because I will invoke the name of the Lord: give ye magnificence to our God.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: The works of God are perfect, and all his ways are judgments: God is faithful and without any iniquity, he is just and right.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: They have sinned against him, and are none of his children in their filth: they are a wicked and perverse generation.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Is this the return thou makest to the Lord, O foolish and senseless people? Is not he thy father, that hath possessed thee, and made thee, and created thee?

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Remember the days of old, think upon every generation: ask thy father, and he will declare to thee: thy elders and they will tell thee.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: When the Most High divided the nations: when he separated the sons of Adam, he appointed the bounds of people according to the number of the children of Israel.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: But the Lord’s portion is his people: Jacob the lot of his inheritance.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: He found him in a desert land, in a place of horror, and of vast wilderness: he led him about, and taught him: and he kept him as the apple of his eye.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: As the eagle enticing her young to fly, and hovering over them, he spread his wings, and hath taken him and carried him on his shoulders.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: The Lord alone was his leader: and there was no strange god with him.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: He set him upon high land: that he might eat the fruits of the fields, that he might suck honey out of the rock, and oil out of the hardest stone,

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Butter of the herd, and milk of the sheep with the fat of lambs, and of the rams of the breed of Basan: and goats with the marrow of wheat, and might drink the purest blood of the grape.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: The beloved grew fat, and kicked: he grew fat, and thick and gross, he forsook God who made him, and departed from God his saviour.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: They provoked him by strange gods, and stirred him up to anger, with their abominations.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: They sacrificed to devils and not to God: to gods whom they knew not: that were newly come up, whom their fathers worshipped not.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Thou hast forsaken the God that begot thee, and hast forgotten the Lord that created thee.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: The Lord saw, and was moved to wrath: because his own sons and daughters provoked him.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And he said: I will hide my face from them, and will consider what their last end shall be: for it is a perverse generation, and unfaithful children.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: They have provoked me with that which was no god, and have angered me with their vanities: and I will provoke them with that which is no people, and will vex them with a foolish nation.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: A fire is kindled in my wrath, and shall burn even to the lowest hell: and shall devour the earth with her increase, and shall burn the foundations of the mountains.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: I will heap evils upon them, and will spend my arrows among them.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: They shall be consumed with famine, and birds shall devour them with a most bitter bite: I will send the teeth of beasts upon them, with the fury of creatures that trail upon the ground, and of serpents.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Without, the sword shall lay them waste, and terror within, both the young man and the virgin, the sucking child with the man in years.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: I said: Where are they? I will make the memory of them to cease from among men.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: But for the wrath of the enemies I have deferred it: lest perhaps their enemies might be proud, and should say: Our mighty hand, and not the Lord, hath done all these things.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: They are a nation without counsel, and without wisdom.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: O that they would be wise and would understand, and would provide for their last end.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: How should one pursue after a thousand, and two chase ten thousand? Was it not, because their God had sold them, and the Lord had shut them up?

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: For our God is not as their gods: our enemies themselves are judges.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Their vines are of the vineyard of Sodom, and of the suburbs of Gomorrha: their grapes are grapes of gall, and their clusters most bitter.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Their wine is the gall of dragons, and the venom of asps, which is incurable.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Are not these things stored up with me, and sealed up in my treasures?

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Revenge is mine, and I will repay them in due time, that their foot may slide: the day of destruction is at hand, and the time makes haste to come.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: The Lord will judge his people, and will have mercy on his servants: he shall see that their hand is weakened, and that they who were shut up have also failed, and they that remained are consumed.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And he shall say: Where are their gods, in whom they trusted?

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Of whose victims they ate the fat, and drank the wine of their drink offerings: let them arise and help you, and protect you in your distress.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: See ye that I alone am, and there is no other God besides me: I will kill and I will make to live: I will strike, and I will heal, and there is none that can deliver out of my hand.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: I will lift up my hand to heaven, and I will say: I live for ever.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: If I shall whet my sword as the lightning, and my hand take hold on judgment: I will render vengeance to my enemies, and repay them that hate me.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: I will make my arrows drunk with blood, and my sword shall devour flesh, of the blood of the slain and of the captivity, of the bare head of the enemies.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Praise his people, ye nations, for he will revenge the blood of his servants: and will render vengeance to their enemies, and he will be merciful to the land of his people.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: So Moses came and spoke all the words of this canticle in the ears of the people, and Josue the son of Nun.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And he ended all these words, speaking to all Israel.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And he said to them: Set your hearts on all the words, which I testify to you this day: which you shall command your children to observe and to do, and to fulfil all that is written in this law:

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: For they are not commanded you in vain, but that every one should live in them, and that doing them you may continue a long time in the land whither you are going over the Jordan to possess it.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And the Lord spoke to Moses the same day, saying:

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Go up into this mountain Abarim, (that is to say, of passages,) unto mount Nebo, which is in the land of Moab over against Jericho: and see the land of Chanaan, which I will deliver to the children of Israel to possess, and die thou in the mountain.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: When thou art gone up into it thou shalt be gathered to thy people, as Aaron thy brother died in mount Hor, and was gathered to his people:

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Because you trespassed against me in the midst of the children of Israel, at the waters of contradiction, in Cades of the desert of Sin: and you did not sanctify me among the children of Israel.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Thou shalt see the land before thee, which I will give to the children of Israel, but thou shalt not enter into it.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: This is the blessing, wherewith the man of God, Moses, blessed the children of Israel, before his death.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And he said: The Lord came from Sinai, and from Seir he rose up to us: he hath appeared from mount Pharan, and with him thousands of saints. In his right hand a fiery law.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: He hath loved the people, all the saints are in his hand: and they that approach to his feet, shall receive of his doctrine.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Moses commanded us a law, the inheritance of the multitude of Jacob.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: He shall be king with the most right, the princes of the people, being assembled with the tribes of Israel.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Let Ruben live, and not die, and be he small in number.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: This is the blessing of Juda. Hear, O Lord, the voice of Juda, and bring him in unto his people: his hands shall fight for him, and he shall be his helper against his enemies.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: To Levi also he said: Thy perfection, and thy doctrine be to thy holy man, whom thou hast proved in the temptation, and judged at the waters of contradiction:

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Who hath said to his father, and to his mother: I do not know you; and to his brethren: I know you not: and their own children they have not known. These have kept thy word, and observed thy covenant,

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Thy judgments, O Jacob, and thy law, O Israel: they shall put incense in thy wrath and holocaust upon thy altar.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Bless, O Lord, his strength, and receive the works of his hands. Strike the backs of his enemies, and let not them that hate him rise.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And to Benjamin he said: The best beloved of the Lord shall dwell confidently in him: as in a bride chamber shall he abide all the day long, and between his shoulders shall be rest.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: To Joseph also he said: Of the blessing of the Lord be his land, of the fruits of heaven, and of the dew, and of the deep that lieth beneath.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Of the fruits brought forth by the sun and by the moon.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Of the tops of the ancient mountains, of the fruits of the everlasting hills:

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And of the fruits of the earth, and of the fulness thereof. The blessing of him that appeared in the bush, come upon the head of Joseph, and upon the crown of the Nazarite among his brethren.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: His beauty as of the firstling of a bullock, his horns as the horns of a rhinoceros: with them shall he push the nations even to the ends of the earth. These are the multitudes of Ephraim and these the thousands of Manasses.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And to Zabulon he said: Rejoice, O Zabulon, in thy going out; and Issachar in thy tabernacles.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: They shall call the people to the mountain: there shall they sacrifice the victims of justice. Who shall suck as milk the abundance of the sea, and the hidden treasures of the sands.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And to Gad he said: Blessed be Gad in his breadth: he hath rested as a lion, and hath seized upon the arm and the top of the head.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And he saw his pre-eminence, that in his portion the teacher was laid up: who was with the princes of the people, and did the justices of the Lord, and his judgment with Israel.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: To Dan also he said: Dan is a young lion, he shall flow plentifully from Basan.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And To Nephtali he said: Nephtali shall enjoy abundance, and shall be full of the blessings of the Lord: he shall possess the sea and the south.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: To Aser also he said: Let Aser be blessed with children, let him be acceptable to his brethren, and let him dip his foot in oil.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: His shoe shall be iron and brass. As the days of thy youth, so also shall thy old age be.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: There is no other god like the God of the rightest: he that is mounted upon the heaven is thy helper. By his magnificence the clouds run hither and thither.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: His dwelling is above, and underneath are the everlasting arms: he shall cast out the enemy from before thee, and shall say: Be thou brought to nought.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Israel shall dwell in safety, and alone. The eye of Jacob in a land of corn and wine, and the heavens shall be misty with dew.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Blessed art thou, Israel: who is like to thee, O people, that art saved by the Lord? the shield of thy help, and the sword of thy glory: thy enemies shall deny thee, and thou shalt tread upon their necks.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Then Moses went up from the plains of Moab upon mount Nebo, to the top of Phasga over against Jericho: and the Lord shewed him all the land of Galaad as far as Dan.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And all Nephtali, and the land of Ephraim and Manasses, and all the land of Juda unto the furthermost sea,

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And the south part, and the breadth of the plain of Jericho the city of palm trees as far as Segor.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And the Lord said to him: This is the land, for which I swore to Abraham, Isaac, and Jacob, saying: I will give it to thy seed. Thou hast seen it with thy eyes, and shalt not pass over to it.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And Moses the servant of the Lord died there, in the land of Moab, by the commandment of the Lord:

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And he buried him in the valley of the land of Moab over against Phogor: and no man hath known of his sepulchre until this present day.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Moses was a hundred and twenty years old when he died: his eye was not dim, neither were his teeth moved.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And the children of Israel mourned for him in the plains of Moab thirty days: and the days of their mourning in which they mourned Moses were ended.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And Josue the son of Nun was filled with the spirit of wisdom, because Moses had laid his hands upon him. And the children of Israel obeyed him, and did as the Lord commanded Moses.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And there arose no more a prophet in Israel like unto Moses, whom the Lord knew face to face,

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: In all the signs and wonders, which he sent by him, to do in the land of Egypt to Pharao, and to all his servants, and to his whole land,

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And all the mighty hand, and great miracles, which Moses did before all Israel.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner
